phases.name: Phase 2 — Reassess & Cut

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.setup: hold one dumbbell vertically at chest height, cupped in both hands under the top bell. Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out.
exercises.execution: sit back and down, knees tracking over toes, descend until thighs at least parallel to floor. Drive up through mid-foot. Keep chest up, back flat.
exercises.mistakes: knees caving in, heels lifting, rounding lower back, not going deep enough.
exercises.video_search: goblet squat tutorial Jeff Nippard / goblet squat form Squat University
exercises.description:
Setup: hold one dumbbell vertically at chest height, cupped in both hands under the top bell. Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out.
Execution: sit back and down, knees tracking over toes, descend until thighs at least parallel to floor. Drive up through mid-foot. Keep chest up, back flat.
Mistakes: knees caving in, heels lifting, rounding lower back, not going deep enough.
Search: "goblet squat tutorial Jeff Nippard" or "goblet squat form Squat University".

exercises.name: DB Bench Press
exercises.setup: sit on bench with dumbbells on thighs. Lie back, kicking dumbbells up to shoulder level. Feet flat on floor, slight arch in lower back, shoulder blades pulled down and back.
exercises.execution: lower dumbbells under control to chest level, slight bend at elbows (~45° from torso, not flared 90°). Press back up. Do not lock out elbows hard.
exercises.mistakes: elbows flaring 90° (shoulder strain), bouncing off chest, pressing with feet off floor, dumbbells drifting apart at top.
exercises.video_search: dumbbell bench press form Jeff Nippard
exercises.description:
Setup: sit on bench with dumbbells on thighs. Lie back, kicking dumbbells up to shoulder level. Feet flat on floor, slight arch in lower back, shoulder blades pulled down and back.
Execution: lower dumbbells under control to chest level, slight bend at elbows (~45° from torso, not flared 90°). Press back up. Do not lock out elbows hard.
Mistakes: elbows flaring 90° (shoulder strain), bouncing off chest, pressing with feet off floor, dumbbells drifting apart at top.
Search: "dumbbell bench press form Jeff Nippard".

exercises.name: One-Arm DB Row
exercises.setup: right knee and right hand on bench, left foot on floor, back parallel to ground. Left hand holds dumbbell hanging straight down.
exercises.execution: pull dumbbell to hip, elbow driving back and up. Squeeze shoulder blade. Lower under control with full stretch.
exercises.mistakes: rotating torso, using biceps instead of back, short range of motion, jerking the weight.
exercises.description:
Setup: right knee and right hand on bench, left foot on floor, back parallel to ground. Left hand holds dumbbell hanging straight down.
Execution: pull dumbbell to hip, elbow driving back and up. Squeeze shoulder blade. Lower under control with full stretch.
Mistakes: rotating torso, using biceps instead of back, short range of motion, jerking the weight.
Search: "one arm dumbbell row form".

exercises.name: DB Romanian Deadlift
exercises.setup: dumbbells in hands in front of thighs, feet hip-width, slight knee bend (maintained throughout — this is NOT a squat).
exercises.execution: hinge at the hips, pushing hips backwards, lowering dumbbells along the front of your legs. Keep back flat. Descend until you feel a strong hamstring stretch (usually just below the knee for most). Drive hips forward to stand up.
exercises.mistakes: bending knees too much (turns into a squat), rounding lower back, dumbbells drifting away from legs, going too low (below hamstring flexibility).
exercises.description:
Setup: dumbbells in hands in front of thighs, feet hip-width, slight knee bend (maintained throughout — this is NOT a squat).
Execution: hinge at the hips, pushing hips backwards, lowering dumbbells along the front of your legs. Keep back flat. Descend until you feel a strong hamstring stretch (usually just below the knee for most). Drive hips forward to stand up.
Mistakes: bending knees too much (turns into a squat), rounding lower back, dumbbells drifting away from legs, going too low (below hamstring flexibility).
Search: "dumbbell RDL form Jeff Nippard".

exercises.name: DB Overhead Press
exercises.setup: dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing forward, elbows slightly in front of body. Seated on bench with back support is easier for a beginner.
exercises.execution: press dumbbells overhead until arms are straight (do not lock out hard). Lower under control to shoulder level.
exercises.mistakes: leaning back excessively, flaring elbows way out, bouncing at the bottom, pressing behind the head.
exercises.description:
Setup: dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing forward, elbows slightly in front of body. Seated on bench with back support is easier for a beginner.
Execution: press dumbbells overhead until arms are straight (do not lock out hard). Lower under control to shoulder level.
Mistakes: leaning back excessively, flaring elbows way out, bouncing at the bottom, pressing behind the head.
Search: "seated dumbbell shoulder press form".

exercises.name: Chin-Up / Inverted Row
exercises.setup: palms facing you, shoulder-width grip, hang with straight arms.
exercises.execution: pull until chin over bar, squeezing lats and biceps. Lower under control to full hang.
exercises.mistakes: kipping (swinging), half reps, not controlling descent.
exercises.video_search: chin up progression for beginners Jeff Nippard
exercises.description:
If you cannot do a chin-up yet: do inverted rows under a low bar (or use a resistance band assistance). Progress to full chin-ups over weeks.
Chin-up setup: palms facing you, shoulder-width grip, hang with straight arms.
Execution: pull until chin over bar, squeezing lats and biceps. Lower under control to full hang.
Mistakes: kipping (swinging), half reps, not controlling descent.
Search: "chin up progression for beginners Jeff Nippard".

exercises.name: DB Split Squat
exercises.setup: dumbbells at sides, step one foot forward into a long lunge position. Back heel off floor.
exercises.execution: lower straight down until back knee nearly touches floor. Drive up through front heel. Do all reps on one leg, then switch.
exercises.mistakes: leaning too far forward, front knee caving in, pushing off back foot too much (robs the front leg of work).
exercises.description:
Setup: dumbbells at sides, step one foot forward into a long lunge position. Back heel off floor.
Execution: lower straight down until back knee nearly touches floor. Drive up through front heel. Do all reps on one leg, then switch.
Mistakes: leaning too far forward, front knee caving in, pushing off back foot too much (robs the front leg of work).
Search: "dumbbell split squat form".

exercises.name: DB Hip Thrust
exercises.setup: upper back on bench, feet flat on floor, dumbbell across hips (use a pad or towel for comfort). Knees bent ~90° at the top.
exercises.execution: drive hips up until body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders. Squeeze glutes hard at the top. Lower under control.
exercises.mistakes: hyperextending lower back at top, using lower back instead of glutes, not reaching full lockout.
exercises.video_search: dumbbell hip thrust form Bret Contreras
exercises.description:
Setup: upper back on bench, feet flat on floor, dumbbell across hips (use a pad or towel for comfort). Knees bent ~90° at the top.
Execution: drive hips up until body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders. Squeeze glutes hard at the top. Lower under control.
Mistakes: hyperextending lower back at top, using lower back instead of glutes, not reaching full lockout.
Search: "dumbbell hip thrust form Bret Contreras".

exercises.name: DB Deadlift
exercises.setup: dumbbells on floor at sides of feet, feet hip-width, bend at hips and knees to grip them. Back flat, chest up, shoulders slightly in front of dumbbells.
exercises.execution: drive through floor with legs first, then extend hips at the top. Lower by hinging at hips first, then bending knees once dumbbells pass knees.
exercises.mistakes: rounding back, jerking off the floor, hyperextending at top, making it a squat.
exercises.description:
Setup: dumbbells on floor at sides of feet, feet hip-width, bend at hips and knees to grip them. Back flat, chest up, shoulders slightly in front of dumbbells.
Execution: drive through floor with legs first, then extend hips at the top. Lower by hinging at hips first, then bending knees once dumbbells pass knees.
Mistakes: rounding back, jerking off the floor, hyperextending at top, making it a squat.
Search: "dumbbell deadlift form".

exercises.name: DB Incline Press
exercises.setup: bench at 30–45° incline. Same as flat bench otherwise.
exercises.execution: same as flat bench. Slightly more shoulder involvement, slightly less lower pec. Stop just short of lockout.
exercises.mistakes: too steep an incline (becomes a shoulder press), flaring elbows.
exercises.description:
Setup: bench at 30–45° incline. Same as flat bench otherwise.
Execution: same as flat bench. Slightly more shoulder involvement, slightly less lower pec. Stop just short of lockout.
Mistakes: too steep an incline (becomes a shoulder press), flaring elbows.

exercises.name: DB Lateral Raise
exercises.setup: dumbbells at sides, slight elbow bend held throughout.
exercises.execution: raise dumbbells out to sides until arms parallel to floor. Lead with elbows, not hands. Lower slowly (3 seconds down). Use light weight — this is an isolation exercise.
exercises.mistakes: using momentum, going too heavy, shrugging shoulders up.
exercises.description:
Setup: dumbbells at sides, slight elbow bend held throughout.
Execution: raise dumbbells out to sides until arms parallel to floor. Lead with elbows, not hands. Lower slowly (3 seconds down). Use light weight — this is an isolation exercise.
Mistakes: using momentum, going too heavy, shrugging shoulders up.
Search: "dumbbell lateral raise form".

exercises.name: Chest-Supported DB Row
exercises.setup: lie face-down on an incline bench (~30°), dumbbells hanging.
exercises.execution: row both dumbbells up to ribs, squeezing shoulder blades. Control the descent.
exercises.mistakes: lifting chest off bench, short range.
exercises.description:
Setup: lie face-down on an incline bench (~30°), dumbbells hanging.
Execution: row both dumbbells up to ribs, squeezing shoulder blades. Control the descent.
Mistakes: lifting chest off bench, short range.

exercises.name: DB Curl / Hammer Curl
exercises.setup: standing, dumbbells at sides. Palms up for curl; palms facing each other for hammer curl.
exercises.execution: curl up, keeping elbows tucked at sides. Control the descent. Full range.
exercises.mistakes: swinging body to lift the weight, elbows drifting forward.
exercises.description:
Setup: standing, dumbbells at sides. Palms up for curl; palms facing each other for hammer curl.
Execution: curl up, keeping elbows tucked at sides. Control the descent. Full range.
Mistakes: swinging body to lift the weight, elbows drifting forward.

exercises.name: DB Triceps Extension / Skullcrusher
exercises.mistakes: flaring elbows, using shoulders to move the weight.
exercises.description:
Extension (standing, 2 hands, dumbbell behind head): lower dumbbell behind head until forearms parallel to floor, press back up. Elbows stay narrow and point forward.
Skullcrusher (lying on bench): hold two dumbbells arms extended above you, lower by bending at elbows only until dumbbells near forehead, press back up.
Mistakes: flaring elbows, using shoulders to move the weight.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.description: Forearms on floor, body in straight line, core braced, glutes squeezed. Hold for time (30–60 s). Do not let hips sag or pike up.

exercises.name: Hanging Knee Raise
exercises.description: Hang from pull-up bar, controlled raise of knees to chest, controlled lower. No swinging.

guides.phase0:
Action Plan: Hypertrophy & Recomposition Project

*Jamie | Start date: 07/04/2026 | Companion: literature\_review\.docx, evidence\_table\.xlsx*

This plan operationalises the literature review into phase\-by\-phase actions, owners, success criteria, and decision gates\. Each phase has clinical, sleep, nutrition, training, supplement, and tracking workstreams\. Tick boxes are intentionally manual — copy into a journal or print\.

# Phase 0 — Diagnose & Stabilise \(Weeks 1–6\)

Goal: characterise sleep, complete endocrine bloods, embed training and nutrition habits at maintenance, lock in smoking quit\. Phase 0 is information\-gathering plus habit\-building, not a hypertrophy push\.

Exit criteria \(all must be true to advance to Phase 1\):

- Endocrine bloods returned and reviewed with GP\.
- STOP\-BANG completed; if ≥3, sleep study booked or completed\.
- Smoking quit ≥4 weeks, no relapse\.
- Galaxy Watch sleep regularity index improving \(sleep midpoint SD <90 min over 14 days\)\.
- Training adherence ≥80% \(≥10 of 12 sessions completed\)\.
- Body weight stable within ±0\.5 kg over final 14 days\.

## Part A — Clinical Workstream

### Week 1

1. Book GP appointment \(in\-person preferred\)\. Stated reasons: longstanding gynaecomastia, smoking cessation support, snoring with witnessed apnoea, request for baseline bloods\.
2. Prepare a one\-page brief for GP listing: gynaecomastia since puberty, current presentation \(mild fatty \+ suspected glandular\), sleep concerns, training plan starting\.

### At GP appointment

1. Request endocrine panel: total testosterone, free testosterone \(or SHBG to calculate\), oestradiol \(sensitive assay\), LH, FSH, prolactin, hCG, TSH, free T4, LFTs \(ALT/AST/GGT\), U&Es, full lipid panel, HbA1c, ferritin, vitamin D \(25\-OH\), full blood count\.
2. Request STOP\-BANG screening; if score ≥3, request referral for home sleep apnoea test \(or polysomnography\)\.
3. Discuss smoking cessation: NRT, varenicline, or behavioural support as per local pathway\.
4. Ask about clinical exam of chest tissue for glandular vs fatty differentiation; ultrasound only if clinically indicated\.

### Weeks 2–6 \(post\-appointment\)

- Bloods drawn \(fasting, morning ~08:00 for testosterone — most reliable\)\.
- Follow up on results within 2 weeks; copy taken for own records\.
- If sleep study referral made: chase appointment; private home sleep test \(~£150–250\) if NHS wait >6 weeks\.
- Decision gate: if total testosterone <12 nmol/L on two readings AND symptomatic, discuss TRT pathway with GP — but only after sleep is corrected \(sleep is upstream of testosterone\)\.

## Part B — Sleep Workstream

### Week 1

1. Enable Galaxy Watch sleep tracking \+ nocturnal SpO2 monitoring tonight\.
2. Set fixed wake time \(e\.g\. 07:00\) — same every day, weekends included, ±30 min maximum\.
3. Eliminate caffeine after 12:00\.
4. Phone out of bedroom from 22:30; use a basic alarm clock\.
5. Bedroom: <19°C, blackout, no screens in the final 60 min\.

### Weeks 2–6

- Maintain fixed wake time; let bedtime drift naturally\. Track adherence in journal\.
- Log Galaxy Watch data weekly: average TST, sleep midpoint, midpoint SD, SpO2 minimum, any dips <90%\.
- At week 4, share 2\-week dataset for analysis pass\.
- Red\-flag triggers \(escalate to GP\): SpO2 dips <88%, frequent dips below 90%, witnessed apnoeas reported by partner, daytime sleepiness Epworth ≥10\.

## Part C — Smoking Cessation

### Week 1

1. Set quit date \(within 7 days\)\.
2. Choose support modality: NRT \(patch \+ lozenge\), varenicline \(prescription\), or behavioural \(NHS Stop Smoking Service / Better Health Quit Smoking app\)\.
3. Identify trigger contexts \(post\-meal, alcohol, stress\) and pre\-plan substitutions\.

### Weeks 2–6

- Track daily: cigarettes \(target zero\), cravings 0–10, mood 0–10, sleep quality 0–10\.
- Expect sleep disruption weeks 1–4 of withdrawal — do not attribute this to training fatigue\.
- Avoid alcohol weeks 1–4 \(highest relapse trigger\)\.

## Part D — Nutrition \(Maintenance\)

Estimated maintenance: ~2,500–2,700 kcal/day \(refine from scale trend over 14 days\)\.

### Targets

- Calories: maintenance \(adjust ±100 kcal if 14\-day trend drifts >0\.3 kg\)\.
- Protein: 110–145 g/day \(1\.6–2\.2 g/kg\)\. Hard floor 110 g\.
- Distribution: 3–4 meals, 30–45 g protein each\.
- Fibre: 30 g/day minimum\.
- Hydration: 2\.5–3 L/day water\.

### Setup

1. Install a tracker \(MacroFactor, Cronometer, or MyFitnessPal\)\. Log everything for 14 days minimum to calibrate\.
2. Build a meal template: 4 staple breakfasts, lunches, dinners that hit protein target\.
3. Stock protein\-dense staples: chicken/turkey breast, lean mince \(5%\), Greek yoghurt 0%, cottage cheese, eggs, tinned tuna, tofu, whey protein\.

## Part E — Training \(Phase 0 Programme\)

Format: full\-body 3×/week \(Mon/Wed/Fri\)\. RIR 2–3 throughout\. Focus is technique acquisition and habit formation, not maximum effort\.

### Session A \(Mon\)

- Goblet squat 3×8–12
- DB bench press 3×8–12
- One\-arm DB row 3×8–12 each
- DB Romanian deadlift 3×8–12
- Plank 3×30–45 s

### Session B \(Wed\)

- DB split squat 3×10/leg
- DB overhead press 3×8–12
- Chin\-up or inverted row 3×AMRAP
- DB hip thrust 3×12–15
- Hanging knee raise 2×AMRAP

### Session C \(Fri\)

- DB deadlift 3×6–10
- DB incline press 3×8–12
- Chest\-supported DB row 3×10–12
- DB lateral raise 2×12–15
- DB curl \+ triceps extension superset 2×10–15

Progression: add 1 rep per week until top of range; then add ~2 kg and reset to bottom of range\. Log every set in a notebook or app \(Strong, Hevy\)\.

## Part F — Supplements \(Phase 0\)

- Creatine monohydrate 5 g/day \(any time, with water\)\.
- Vitamin D3 25 µg \(1000 IU\)/day until blood level confirmed; adjust per result\.
- Whey protein only if needed to hit protein target\.
- Nothing else\. No pre\-workouts, no peptides, no SERMs, no test boosters\.

## Part G — Tracking & Equipment Setup \(Phase 0\)

### Week 1 purchases

- Adjustable dumbbells 2×32 kg \(~£200–300\)
- Adjustable bench FID/FI \(~£100–200\)
- Doorway or wall\-mounted pull\-up bar \(~£20–80\)
- Body\-weight scale, food scale, tape measure \(~£40\)
- Training notebook or app subscription \(~£0–30\)

### Daily tracking minimum

- Body weight \(morning, post\-void, pre\-food\)
- Sleep duration & wake time
- Cigarettes \(target 0\)
- Protein total

### Weekly tracking

- 7\-day weight average
- Waist circumference at navel
- Training session count \+ adherence %
- Photos: front/side/back, same lighting, same time of day, Sunday morning

# Phase 1 — Lean Bulk \(Weeks 7–26, ~5 months\)

Goal: maximise newbie\-gains hypertrophy on a controlled surplus\. Trigger to enter: all Phase 0 exit criteria met\. If sleep study positive for OSA, Phase 1 begins only after CPAP titration is in progress\.

Exit criteria \(advance to Phase 2\):

- \+3–5 kg body weight gained over the phase\.
- Visible muscle development on photos at week 13 vs week 1\.
- Strength roughly doubled on key compounds \(e\.g\. DB bench from ~12 kg to ~22 kg per hand\)\.
- Sleep regularity maintained; no recurrence of smoking\.
- Body fat estimate \(waist proxy\) within tolerable range — waist gain ≤4 cm from Phase 0 baseline\.

## Part A — Nutrition \(Lean Bulk\)

- Calories: maintenance \+ 150–250 kcal/day\.
- Protein: 110–145 g/day \(no change\)\.
- Target weight gain: 0\.25–0\.35 kg/week \(1\.0–1\.4 kg/month\)\.
- Adjustment rule: if 14\-day trend >0\.4 kg/week → cut 100 kcal\. If <0\.15 kg/week → add 100 kcal\.
- Re\-calibrate every 2 weeks based on rolling 14\-day weight average, not daily readings\.

## Part B — Training \(Phase 1 Programme: Upper/Lower 4×/week\)

### Upper A \(Mon\)

- DB bench press 3×6–10
- One\-arm DB row 3×8–12 each
- DB overhead press 3×8–12
- Chin\-up or inverted row 3×AMRAP
- DB curl 2×10–15
- DB triceps extension 2×10–15

### Lower A \(Tue\)

- Goblet or DB front squat 3×8–12
- DB Romanian deadlift 3×8–12
- DB split squat 3×10/leg
- DB calf raise 3×12–20
- Hanging knee raise 3×AMRAP

### Upper B \(Thu\)

- DB incline press 3×8–12
- Chin\-up or DB pullover 3×8–12
- DB lateral raise 3×12–20
- Chest\-supported DB row 3×10–15
- DB hammer curl 2×10–15
- DB skullcrusher 2×10–15

### Lower B \(Fri\)

- DB deadlift 3×6–10
- Front\-foot\-elevated split squat 3×8–12/leg
- DB hip thrust 3×12–20
- Standing DB calf raise 3×12–20
- Plank 3×45 s

Progression: same as Phase 0 — add reps within range, then add load\. RIR 1–2 on most sets; last set of primary movements occasionally to failure \(every ~10 days\)\.

Deload: at week 13, run a deload week — same exercises, ~60% of usual volume, RIR 4\. Then resume\.

## Part C — Supplements \(Phase 1\)

Same as Phase 0\. No additions\. Re\-evaluate at end of Phase 1 only if a specific deficiency is identified\.

## Part D — Tracking \(Phase 1\)

- All Phase 0 tracking continues\.
- Add: weekly waist measurement at navel\.
- Add: monthly photos in standardised conditions\.
- Add: monthly strength check on 4 key lifts \(DB bench, DB row, goblet squat, DB RDL\) — record top set\.

## Part E — Decision Gates \(Phase 1\)

- Week 10 review: if weight gain >0\.5 kg/week sustained, or waist >3 cm gained → cut surplus by 150 kcal\.
- Week 13 review: if no visible progress on photos AND no strength gain → audit \(sleep, calories actually hitting, protein actually hitting, RIR honesty, sessions completed\)\.
- Week 20 review: assess phase exit criteria\. If body fat clearly creeping up \(waist \+4 cm vs Phase 0\), shorten Phase 1 and move to Phase 2 cut\.

## Part F — Gynaecomastia Reassessment

Booked at end of Phase 1 \(week 26\): self\-examine in good lighting, photos, document any change\. If glandular component still palpable as a discrete disc unchanged from baseline — that is the expected finding for established disease, and the rational next step is a plastic surgery consultation in Phase 2 for information\-gathering only\.

# Phase 2 — Reassess & Cut \(Weeks 27–38, ~3 months\)

Goal: reveal the muscle built in Phase 1 by reducing body fat moderately, while preserving lean mass\. Also: surgical consult on the chest if indicated\.

Trigger: Phase 1 exit criteria met OR waist drift forces an early move\.

## Part A — Nutrition \(Mini\-Cut\)

- Calories: maintenance −300 to −400 kcal/day \(~15% deficit\)\.
- Protein: increase to upper end 130–150 g/day \(slightly higher proportion to preserve LBM in deficit\)\.
- Target rate of loss: 0\.4–0\.6 kg/week \(~0\.6–0\.9% bodyweight/week\)\.
- Duration: 8–12 weeks max\. Do not extend\.
- Refeed: every 7–10 days, eat at maintenance for one day to support training output and adherence\.

## Part B — Training \(Phase 2\)

Same upper/lower split as Phase 1\. Volume reduced by ~20% \(drop one set per exercise on accessories\)\. Loads maintained — the goal is to keep the strength built during the bulk\. Expect strength to plateau, not decline; if it declines >10% on a key lift, slow the cut\.

## Part C — Tracking \(Phase 2\)

- Daily weight \+ protein\.
- Weekly waist \+ photos\.
- Bi\-weekly strength check on 4 key lifts\.
- At week 8 of cut: full reassessment\.

## Part D — Gynaecomastia Surgical Consult \(if indicated\)

1. At end of Phase 2 \(lower body fat = better assessment\), book consultation with a plastic surgeon experienced in gynaecomastia\. UK options: BAAPS\-listed surgeons or NHS referral pathway via GP if symptomatic\.
2. Bring: photos baseline → Phase 1 → Phase 2, endocrine bloods, any sleep study results\.
3. Goals of consult: information\-gathering only\. Discuss subcutaneous mastectomy ± liposuction, recurrence rates, recovery timeline, cost \(UK private ~£3,500–6,500\), training implications \(~6–12 weeks off chest training post\-op\)\.
4. No commitment to surgery during consult\. Decide separately after a cooling\-off period\.

# Phase 3 — Year 2\+ Maintenance & Sustainable Progression

Goal: continue progressing slowly without the urgency of newbie gains\. By month 12, the easy gains are largely captured; the next gains are slower, more variable, and more dependent on consistency\.

## Year 2 framework

- Alternate 4–6 month lean\-bulk blocks with 8–12 week mini\-cuts\.
- Re\-test bloods annually\.
- Re\-evaluate equipment ceiling: by year 2 you may need a barbell \+ rack \+ plates as DB progression caps out\.
- Skill expansion: introduce variations \(single\-leg work, dip station, etc\.\) to extend exercise pool\.

## Long\-term flags to revisit

- TRT decision \(only if symptomatic with confirmed low testosterone after sleep \+ lifestyle correction\)\.
- GLP\-1 \+ activin combination therapies \(monitor literature; potentially relevant for future cuts\)\.
- Bimagrumab/myostatin pathway approval status \(monthly auto\-refresh\)\.
- Gynaecomastia surgery: revisit if not already done, especially if year\-2 photos show the chest as the limiting aesthetic factor\.

# Appendix A — Weekly Routine Summary

Mon: Upper A | track weight, sleep, protein

Tue: Lower A | track weight, sleep, protein

Wed: Rest or 20 min walk | track weight, sleep, protein

Thu: Upper B | track weight, sleep, protein

Fri: Lower B | track weight, sleep, protein

Sat: Rest or active recovery | photos, waist measurement \(Sunday morning\)

Sun: Rest | review week, plan next, weigh\-in average

# Appendix B — Red Flags Requiring Escalation

- SpO2 dips <88% on Galaxy Watch overnight → push for sleep study urgently\.
- Resting heart rate sustained >85 bpm without obvious cause → GP review\.
- Unexplained weight loss in lean bulk → audit \+ GP review if persistent\.
- Persistent low mood, anhedonia, suicidal ideation → contact GP / Samaritans 116 123\. Smoking cessation can unmask depression\.
- Chest pain or breathlessness during training → stop, seek urgent medical assessment\.
- Joint pain that does not resolve in 7 days → reduce load, consider physio\.

# Appendix C — What This Plan Deliberately Excludes

Peptides \(BPC\-157, TB\-500, GH secretagogues\): no human evidence for hypertrophy, grey\-market sourcing\.

SARMs: suppressive despite marketing claims; fail your stated criteria\.

SERMs \(tamoxifen, raloxifene, clomiphene\) for muscle: no evidence base; mechanism does not equal clinical effect\.

GLP\-1 agonists in current phase: lean mass cost opposes goals at BMI 22\.

Ashwagandha, beta\-alanine, citrulline, BCAAs, HMB, urolithin A: weak evidence, not worth the budget in year 1\.

Test boosters of any kind: no evidence base\.

# Appendix D — Citation Pointers

Full citations and tier/GRADE ratings are in the companion file evidence\_table\.xlsx\. Key references for this plan:

- Morton 2018 — protein dose\-response \(PMID 28698222\)
- Schoenfeld 2017 — volume dose\-response \(PMID 27102172\)
- Refalo 2024 — proximity to failure \(PMID 38970765\)
- Longland 2016 — high\-protein deficit recomp \(PMID 26817506\)
- Leproult 2011 — sleep restriction & testosterone \(PMID 21632481\)
- Heymsfield 2021 — bimagrumab phase 2 \(PMID 33439265\)
guides.sleep:
Clinical & Sleep Guide

*Jamie | Companion to action\_plan\.docx*

This is the clinical workstream — GP visit, bloods, sleep investigation, smoking cessation, and gynaecomastia pathway\. Read once, action the items with deadlines\.

# 1\. The GP Visit \(Book This Week\)

This is the single most important action in Phase 0\. Everything downstream benefits from the information it generates\. Book a double appointment if possible \(15–20 minutes\) — you have four things to cover\.

__Four things to raise at the GP visit:__

1. Longstanding gynaecomastia \(since puberty, >15 years\) — request clinical exam and endocrine bloods to rule out reversible causes\.
2. Snoring with partner\-observed apnoea episodes — request STOP\-BANG screening and sleep study referral if indicated\.
3. Smoking cessation support — discuss NRT, varenicline, or behavioural pathway\.
4. General baseline health check since starting structured training and dietary changes\.

__Bloods to request \(copy\-paste this list into a note for the GP\):__

- Total testosterone \(morning, fasting, ~08:00–10:00 — circadian rhythm matters\)
- Free testosterone OR SHBG \(so free can be calculated\)
- Oestradiol — sensitive assay if available
- LH \(luteinising hormone\)
- FSH \(follicle\-stimulating hormone\)
- Prolactin
- hCG \(rules out rare testicular germ cell tumour — standard in gynaecomastia workup\)
- TSH and free T4 \(thyroid\)
- LFTs \(ALT, AST, GGT, bilirubin — liver\)
- U&Es \(kidney function and electrolytes\)
- HbA1c \(glucose regulation\)
- Full lipid panel \(total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, triglycerides\)
- Ferritin \(iron stores\)
- 25\-OH vitamin D
- Full blood count

Note: NHS GPs do not always run every item on request\. If any are declined, the highest\-priority ones for your situation are: total testosterone, oestradiol, LH, FSH, prolactin, hCG, TSH, LFTs, vitamin D\. Private bloods \(Medichecks, Thriva, Forth\) are ~£80–150 if you want to fill gaps and are a reasonable investment once\.

# 2\. Interpreting the Bloods \(What to Look For\)

Ask the GP for a copy of all results\. Key reference ranges \(UK\):

- Total testosterone: 8\.6–29 nmol/L\. Lab\-dependent\. <12 nmol/L combined with symptoms may indicate hypogonadism — discuss with GP, consider repeat\.
- LH: 1\.7–8\.6 IU/L\. Elevated with low testosterone = primary hypogonadism \(testicular\)\. Normal/low with low testosterone = secondary hypogonadism \(pituitary/hypothalamic\)\.
- FSH: 1\.5–12\.4 IU/L\. Similar interpretation pattern\.
- Oestradiol \(men\): 40–180 pmol/L roughly\. Elevated oestradiol relative to testosterone contributes to gynaecomastia\.
- Prolactin: <325 mIU/L in men\. Elevated prolactin suppresses testosterone and can cause gynaecomastia — prolactinoma must be excluded if high\.
- hCG: should be undetectable in healthy men\. Detectable hCG requires urgent workup for testicular tumour\.
- TSH: 0\.4–4\.0 mU/L\. Both hyper\- and hypothyroidism can contribute to gynaecomastia\.
- Vitamin D: aim for 50–125 nmol/L\. <25 nmol/L = deficient, 25–50 = insufficient\. Supplement accordingly\.

If anything is clearly abnormal, discuss with GP before acting\. Do not self\-interpret and start supplements or drugs based on borderline numbers\.

# 3\. Sleep Investigation Pathway

__STOP\-BANG screening \(do this yourself first, bring score to GP\):__

Score 1 point for each YES answer:

- S — Snoring loudly \(loud enough to be heard through a closed door\)?
- T — Tired/fatigued during the day, or falling asleep when you should not?
- O — Observed to stop breathing, choke, or gasp during sleep?
- P — High blood pressure \(treated or untreated\)?
- B — BMI > 35?
- A — Age > 50?
- N — Neck circumference > 43 cm \(17 inches\)?
- G — Male sex?

Score interpretation: 0–2 low risk, 3–4 intermediate risk, 5–8 high risk\. You are likely 2–4 based on what you have told me \(snoring, observed apnoea, male — plus possibly tired\)\. Any score ≥3 warrants further investigation\.

__Sleep study options:__

- NHS pathway: GP referral → respiratory/sleep clinic → home sleep apnoea test \(HSAT\) in most cases, polysomnography for complex cases\. Wait times 6–16 weeks depending on region\.
- Private home sleep test: ~£150–250 \(e\.g\. Intus, Sleep Clinic, Bupa\) — turnaround 1–2 weeks\.
- Galaxy Watch SpO2 data is not diagnostic but useful: persistent nocturnal dips below 90% SpO2 strengthen the case for formal testing\.

__What happens if OSA is diagnosed:__

Severity is graded by AHI \(apnoea\-hypopnoea index\) — events per hour of sleep\. Mild 5–14, moderate 15–29, severe ≥30\. Treatment:

- Mild: lifestyle \(weight, smoking, alcohol, sleep position\) ± mandibular advancement device\.
- Moderate\-severe: CPAP is standard\. Well\-tolerated after adjustment period\. Meaningfully improves sleep quality, daytime function, cardiovascular risk, and modestly improves testosterone in some studies\.

# 4\. Sleep Regularity Protocol \(Start Tonight\)

Sleep regularity — consistent timing — outperforms sleep duration as a health predictor in recent large cohort studies\. This is the highest\-leverage free intervention\.

1. Pick a fixed wake time \(e\.g\. 07:00\)\. Stick to it ±30 minutes every day including weekends for 6 weeks\.
2. Let bedtime drift naturally — when you are tired, go to bed\. Do not force an early bedtime while adjusting\.
3. Light exposure: get outside or by a bright window within 30 minutes of waking\. 10 minutes is enough\.
4. Caffeine cutoff: no caffeine after 12:00\. Half\-life is ~5–7 hours; afternoon coffee still has meaningful levels at midnight\.
5. No alcohol during the quit\-smoking window \(relapse trigger and sleep disruptor\)\.
6. Phone out of bedroom from 22:30\. Basic alarm clock instead\.
7. Bedroom <19°C, blackout, quiet\.
8. No screens in the final 60 minutes before bed\. Read a physical book, shower, stretch\.
9. If you cannot sleep after 20 minutes in bed: get up, do something boring in dim light, return when sleepy\.

# 5\. Galaxy Watch Setup and Data Collection

1. Enable sleep tracking in Samsung Health app → Sleep\.
2. Enable continuous SpO2 monitoring during sleep: Samsung Health → Sleep → Sleep coaching or Advanced settings → Blood oxygen during sleep = ON\.
3. Wear the watch snugly every night \(looser during day, snug at night for accurate SpO2\)\.
4. After 14 nights: export data\. Samsung Health → More → Settings → Download personal data, or take screenshots of the weekly sleep view\.
5. Share the data with me for a proper analysis pass: total sleep time, midpoint, regularity, SpO2 minimums and dips\.

# 6\. Smoking Cessation Plan

__Week 1 — setup:__

1. Set a quit date within 7 days\.
2. Choose support: \(a\) NRT patches \+ lozenges for breakthrough cravings, ~£30/week, available OTC or free via NHS Stop Smoking Service; \(b\) varenicline \(Champix\) — prescription from GP, stronger efficacy, some side effects to discuss; \(c\) NHS Quit Smoking app \(Better Health\) for behavioural support\.
3. Identify your trigger contexts: morning coffee, after meals, stress, alcohol, social situations\. Write them down\.
4. Plan substitutions for each trigger: gum, cold water, 5\-minute walk, deep breathing\.
5. Tell one person \(partner, friend\) you are quitting and ask for support\.

__Weeks 1–4 — active withdrawal:__

- Expect: disrupted sleep, irritability, increased appetite, low mood, difficulty concentrating\. These peak at days 3–5 and ease substantially by week 3\.
- Track daily: cigarettes \(target 0\), cravings 0–10, mood 0–10, sleep quality 0–10\.
- Avoid alcohol entirely — highest relapse trigger\.
- Exercise helps with cravings — a 5\-minute walk can break a strong urge\.
- If you slip: it is a slip, not a failure\. Get back on immediately\. Most successful quitters had multiple attempts\.

__Red flags \(see GP\):__

- Persistent low mood or suicidal thoughts \(varenicline has a black\-box warning, NRT less so; either can unmask underlying depression\)\.
- Severe insomnia lasting >3 weeks\.
- Palpitations or chest pain\.

# 7\. Gynaecomastia — What to Expect

Honest framing: the literature on established \(>12 months, fibrotic phase\) gynaecomastia is clear that non\-surgical interventions — fat loss, training, SERMs, supplements, aromatase inhibitors — do not resolve the glandular component\. They can improve the surrounding pseudogynaecomastia \(fat\) and alter the overall silhouette, but the discrete disc of glandular tissue under the areola is fibrotic and will not regress without surgery\.

What Phase 0–1 achieves on the chest:

- Clarifies the pseudo vs glandular split as body composition changes\.
- Builds pec development that improves the overall chest silhouette\.
- Generates endocrine data that rules out reversible causes\.

What Phase 0–1 does NOT achieve on the chest:

- Does not eliminate the glandular disc if it is fibrotic \(very likely in a 15\+ year case\)\.
- May make the glandular component more visible at very lean body fat \(no fat to mask it\)\.

Surgical consultation, if the above is still the binding aesthetic issue at end of Phase 1:

- Find a BAAPS\- or BAPRAS\-listed plastic surgeon with specific gynaecomastia volume\. Ask about their annual case count\.
- Standard procedure: subcutaneous mastectomy ± liposuction under general anaesthetic, day case\.
- UK private cost: typically £3,500–6,500\.
- Recovery: compression vest 4–6 weeks; no chest training 6–12 weeks; final result at 3–6 months\.
- Complications \(rare\): contour irregularity, nipple sensitivity change, haematoma, infection, asymmetry\. Revision rate reported 5–15% depending on series\.
- Patient satisfaction in published case series is generally high \(T4 evidence\)\.

The consultation is information\-gathering only\. Do not commit on the day\. Take two weeks minimum before deciding\.

- hCG: should be undetectable in healthy men\. Detectable hCG requires urgent workup for testicular tumour\.
- TSH: 0\.4–4\.0 mU/L\. Both hyper\- and hypothyroidism can contribute to gynaecomastia\.
- Vitamin D: aim for 50–125 nmol/L\. <25 nmol/L = deficient, 25–50 = insufficient\. Supplement accordingly\.

If anything is clearly abnormal, discuss with GP before acting\. Do not self\-interpret and start supplements or drugs based on borderline numbers\.

# 3\. Sleep Investigation Pathway

__STOP\-BANG screening \(do this yourself first, bring score to GP\):__

Score 1 point for each YES answer:

- S — Snoring loudly \(loud enough to be heard through a closed door\)?
- T — Tired/fatigued during the day, or falling asleep when you should not?
- O — Observed to stop breathing, choke, or gasp during sleep?
- P — High blood pressure \(treated or untreated\)?
- B — BMI > 35?
- A — Age > 50?
- N — Neck circumference > 43 cm \(17 inches\)?
- G — Male sex?

Score interpretation: 0–2 low risk, 3–4 intermediate risk, 5–8 high risk\. You are likely 2–4 based on what you have told me \(snoring, observed apnoea, male — plus possibly tired\)\. Any score ≥3 warrants further investigation\.

__Sleep study options:__

- NHS pathway: GP referral → respiratory/sleep clinic → home sleep apnoea test \(HSAT\) in most cases, polysomnography for complex cases\. Wait times 6–16 weeks depending on region\.
- Private home sleep test: ~£150–250 \(e\.g\. Intus, Sleep Clinic, Bupa\) — turnaround 1–2 weeks\.
- Galaxy Watch SpO2 data is not diagnostic but useful: persistent nocturnal dips below 90% SpO2 strengthen the case for formal testing\.

__What happens if OSA is diagnosed:__

Severity is graded by AHI \(apnoea\-hypopnoea index\) — events per hour of sleep\. Mild 5–14, moderate 15–29, severe ≥30\. Treatment:

- Mild: lifestyle \(weight, smoking, alcohol, sleep position\) ± mandibular advancement device\.
- Moderate\-severe: CPAP is standard\. Well\-tolerated after adjustment period\. Meaningfully improves sleep quality, daytime function, cardiovascular risk, and modestly improves testosterone in some studies\.

# 4\. Sleep Regularity Protocol \(Start Tonight\)

Sleep regularity — consistent timing — outperforms sleep duration as a health predictor in recent large cohort studies\. This is the highest\-leverage free intervention\.

1. Pick a fixed wake time \(e\.g\. 07:00\)\. Stick to it ±30 minutes every day including weekends for 6 weeks\.
2. Let bedtime drift naturally — when you are tired, go to bed\. Do not force an early bedtime while adjusting\.
3. Light exposure: get outside or by a bright window within 30 minutes of waking\. 10 minutes is enough\.
4. Caffeine cutoff: no caffeine after 12:00\. Half\-life is ~5–7 hours; afternoon coffee still has meaningful levels at midnight\.
5. No alcohol during the quit\-smoking window \(relapse trigger and sleep disruptor\)\.
6. Phone out of bedroom from 22:30\. Basic alarm clock instead\.
7. Bedroom <19°C, blackout, quiet\.
8. No screens in the final 60 minutes before bed\. Read a physical book, shower, stretch\.
9. If you cannot sleep after 20 minutes in bed: get up, do something boring in dim light, return when sleepy\.

# 5\. Galaxy Watch Setup and Data Collection

1. Enable sleep tracking in Samsung Health app → Sleep\.
2. Enable continuous SpO2 monitoring during sleep: Samsung Health → Sleep → Sleep coaching or Advanced settings → Blood oxygen during sleep = ON\.
3. Wear the watch snugly every night \(looser during day, snug at night for accurate SpO2\)\.
4. After 14 nights: export data\. Samsung Health → More → Settings → Download personal data, or take screenshots of the weekly sleep view\.
5. Share the data with me for a proper analysis pass: total sleep time, midpoint, regularity, SpO2 minimums and dips\.

# 6\. Smoking Cessation Plan

__Week 1 — setup:__

1. Set a quit date within 7 days\.
2. Choose support: \(a\) NRT patches \+ lozenges for breakthrough cravings, ~£30/week, available OTC or free via NHS Stop Smoking Service; \(b\) varenicline \(Champix\) — prescription from GP, stronger efficacy, some side effects to discuss; \(c\) NHS Quit Smoking app \(Better Health\) for behavioural support\.
3. Identify your trigger contexts: morning coffee, after meals, stress, alcohol, social situations\. Write them down\.
4. Plan substitutions for each trigger: gum, cold water, 5\-minute walk, deep breathing\.
5. Tell one person \(partner, friend\) you are quitting and ask for support\.

__Weeks 1–4 — active withdrawal:__

- Expect: disrupted sleep, irritability, increased appetite, low mood, difficulty concentrating\. These peak at days 3–5 and ease substantially by week 3\.
- Track daily: cigarettes \(target 0\), cravings 0–10, mood 0–10, sleep quality 0–10\.
- Avoid alcohol entirely — highest relapse trigger\.
- Exercise helps with cravings — a 5\-minute walk can break a strong urge\.
- If you slip: it is a slip, not a failure\. Get back on immediately\. Most successful quitters had multiple attempts\.

__Red flags \(see GP\):__

- Persistent low mood or suicidal thoughts \(varenicline has a black\-box warning, NRT less so; either can unmask underlying depression\)\.
- Severe insomnia lasting >3 weeks\.
- Palpitations or chest pain\.

# 7\. Gynaecomastia — What to Expect

Honest framing: the literature on established \(>12 months, fibrotic phase\) gynaecomastia is clear that non\-surgical interventions — fat loss, training, SERMs, supplements, aromatase inhibitors — do not resolve the glandular component\. They can improve the surrounding pseudogynaecomastia \(fat\) and alter the overall silhouette, but the discrete disc of glandular tissue under the areola is fibrotic and will not regress without surgery\.

What Phase 0–1 achieves on the chest:

- Clarifies the pseudo vs glandular split as body composition changes\.
- Builds pec development that improves the overall chest silhouette\.
- Generates endocrine data that rules out reversible causes\.

What Phase 0–1 does NOT achieve on the chest:

- Does not eliminate the glandular disc if it is fibrotic \(very likely in a 15\+ year case\)\.
- May make the glandular component more visible at very lean body fat \(no fat to mask it\)\.

Surgical consultation, if the above is still the binding aesthetic issue at end of Phase 1:

- Find a BAAPS\- or BAPRAS\-listed plastic surgeon with specific gynaecomastia volume\. Ask about their annual case count\.
- Standard procedure: subcutaneous mastectomy ± liposuction under general anaesthetic, day case\.
- UK private cost: typically £3,500–6,500\.
- Recovery: compression vest 4–6 weeks; no chest training 6–12 weeks; final result at 3–6 months\.
- Complications \(rare\): contour irregularity, nipple sensitivity change, haematoma, infection, asymmetry\. Revision rate reported 5–15% depending on series\.
- Patient satisfaction in published case series is generally high \(T4 evidence\)\.

The consultation is information\-gathering only\. Do not commit on the day\. Take two weeks minimum before deciding\.
guides.nutrition:
Nutrition & Meal Prep Guide

*Jamie | v1 07/04/2026 | Companion to action\_plan\.docx*

Practical "what to eat, when, how to cook it in the Ninja air fryer, how long it lasts in the fridge" layer beneath the action plan\. Fish/seafood excluded per preference\. Numbers are calibration starting points — adjust from the 14\-day weight trend per the tracking guide\.

# 1\. Principles

Four numbers, in priority order:

1. Protein 110–145 g/day\. Hard floor 110 g\. Non\-negotiable\.
2. Calories: Phase 0 maintenance \(~2,500–2,700 kcal\)\. Phase 1 maintenance \+150–250 kcal\.
3. Fibre 30 g/day minimum\.
4. Water 2\.5–3 L/day; \+0\.5 L on training days; creatine adds slightly\.

Fats and carbs are not micro\-managed once protein, calories, and fibre are set\.

# 2\. Daily Targets and Protein Reference

At 65\.8 kg, 1\.6–2\.2 g/kg protein = 105–145 g/day\. Aim 120–130 g across 3–4 meals of 30–40 g \(Schoenfeld & Aragon 2018, ~0\.4 g/kg/meal optimum\)\.

__~30 g protein equivalents:__

- 120 g cooked chicken breast \(~140 g raw\)
- 120 g cooked lean mince 5%
- 150 g cooked steak / pork loin / turkey breast
- 200 g Greek yoghurt 0% \+ 10 g whey
- 200 g cottage cheese
- 5 large eggs \(~30 g protein, ~350 kcal\)
- 1 scoop whey \(25 g\) \+ 250 ml semi\-skimmed milk

# 3\. The Meal System — 8 Air\-Fryer Recipes

Each recipe lists macros per portion, total time, and fridge life\. All cooked in the Ninja air fryer unless noted\. Rotate through the week — pick 2 lunches and 2 dinners to batch on Sunday\.

__Recipe 1 — Peri\-Peri Chicken, Potatoes, Broccoli \(~505 kcal | 48 P | 50 C | 12 F\)__

Per portion: 140 g raw chicken breast, 200 g new potatoes halved, 150 g broccoli florets, 1 tsp olive oil, peri\-peri seasoning, salt\.

1. Toss potatoes in oil \+ salt\. Air fry 200°C, 18 min, shaking once\.
2. Coat chicken in peri\-peri\. Add to drawer with potatoes for the last 12 min, flip halfway\.
3. Steam or microwave broccoli 3 min in the last 3 min\.

Fridge: 4 days\. Reheat: air fryer 180°C 5 min or microwave 2 min covered\.

__Recipe 2 — Turkey Burrito Bowl \(~560 kcal | 42 P | 60 C | 14 F\)__

Per portion: 130 g raw turkey mince, 60 g dry basmati, ½ tin black beans \(drained\), 50 g sweetcorn, 30 g cheddar, salsa, fajita seasoning\.

1. Cook rice on hob \(no air fryer needed\): 12 min simmer \+ 5 min rest\.
2. Brown turkey \+ seasoning in pan 6 min, or air\-fry crumbled in foil tray 200°C 10 min, stir halfway\.
3. Assemble: rice → beans → turkey → sweetcorn → cheese → salsa\.

Fridge: 4 days\. Cheese on top after reheating\.

__Recipe 3 — Steak, Sweet Potato, Asparagus \(~580 kcal | 45 P | 45 C | 22 F\)__

Per portion: 170 g rump or sirloin, 250 g sweet potato cubed, 100 g asparagus, olive oil, salt, pepper, garlic powder\.

1. Sweet potato in air fryer 200°C, 18 min\.
2. Steak: pat dry, season, air fryer 200°C 6 min for medium\-rare \(8 min medium\)\. Rest 5 min\.
3. Asparagus: last 5 min of sweet potato, tossed in oil\.

Fridge: cook fresh; sweet potato keeps 4 days reheated, steak best fresh\.

__Recipe 4 — Pork Loin, Apple Slaw, Rice \(~540 kcal | 44 P | 55 C | 14 F\)__

Per portion: 160 g pork loin, 60 g dry rice, 80 g shredded white cabbage, ½ apple julienned, 1 tbsp Greek yoghurt, 1 tsp Dijon, lemon\.

1. Pork: season, air fryer 190°C 12–14 min, internal 63°C, rest 5 min\.
2. Rice on hob\.
3. Slaw: mix cabbage, apple, yoghurt, Dijon, lemon, salt\.

Fridge: pork \+ rice 4 days\. Make slaw fresh \(keeps 2 days\)\.

__Recipe 5 — Overnight Protein Oats \(~625 kcal | 42 P | 75 C | 14 F\)__

Per portion: 70 g oats, 250 ml semi\-skimmed milk, 1 scoop whey \(25 g protein\), 150 g Greek yoghurt 0%, 1 banana, 10 g honey, cinnamon\.

1. Combine oats, milk, whey, yoghurt, cinnamon in a jar\. Stir\.
2. Refrigerate overnight \(≥6 h\)\.
3. Top with sliced banana \+ honey before eating\.

Fridge: 3 days\. Make 3 jars at once on Sunday\.

__Recipe 6 — Air\-Fryer Egg & Bacon Muffins \(~290 kcal | 24 P | 4 C | 19 F per 3 muffins\)__

Batch of 6: 6 eggs, 6 rashers lean back bacon chopped, 30 g grated cheddar, chives, pepper\.

1. Whisk eggs \+ cheese \+ pepper\.
2. Distribute bacon into 6 silicone muffin cups\.
3. Pour egg mix over\. Air fryer 160°C, 10–12 min until set\.

Fridge: 4 days\. Eat cold or 30 s microwave\. Pair with toast \+ fruit for a full breakfast \(~520 kcal, 32 P\)\.

__Recipe 7 — Cottage Cheese Power Bowl \(~470 kcal | 35 P | 35 C | 18 F\) — no cook__

Per portion: 250 g cottage cheese, 40 g granola, 100 g berries, 15 g almonds, 10 g honey\.

1. Layer in a bowl\. Eat\. 90 seconds\.

Assemble fresh; ingredients keep separately a week\. Default rest\-day lunch or pre\-bed\.

__Recipe 8 — Halloumi & Chicken Wraps \(~620 kcal | 34 P | 55 C | 28 F\)__

Per portion: 80 g halloumi sliced, 80 g cooked chicken \(from Recipe 1 batch\), 1 large wholemeal wrap, 30 g hummus, salad leaves, tomato, red onion, lemon\.

1. Halloumi in air fryer 200°C 6 min, flip halfway\.
2. Warm wrap 20 s in microwave\.
3. Spread hummus, layer chicken, halloumi, salad\. Roll\.

Best fresh; halloumi keeps 3 days cooked\. Good "I have 5 minutes" lunch\.

# 4\. Weekly Rotation and Sunday Prep

Default week: Recipes 1, 2, 3, 4 cover lunches and dinners; Recipe 5 or 6 covers breakfasts; Recipe 7 covers snacks; Recipe 8 is the flexible top\-up\.

__Sunday 90\-minute air\-fryer batch:__

1. 0–10 min: Cook 60 g × 3 = 180 g rice on hob \(covers Recipes 2 \+ 4\)\. Start sweet potato cubes \(500 g\) in air fryer 200°C 18 min\.
2. 10–25 min: While sweet potato runs, season 500 g chicken breast \(peri\-peri\) and 4 portions of pork loin\. Make slaw\.
3. 25–45 min: Air\-fry chicken \(200°C 14 min, in two drawers if needed\)\. Brown turkey mince in pan with fajita seasoning\.
4. 45–65 min: Air\-fry pork loin \(190°C 14 min\)\. Make 6 egg & bacon muffins \(160°C 12 min in second drawer or after pork\)\.
5. 65–80 min: Assemble 4 burrito bowls \+ 4 peri\-peri chicken containers\. Make 3 jars of overnight oats\.
6. 80–90 min: Wash up\. Label containers with date\.

Wednesday 20\-min top\-up: Fresh steak \(Recipe 3\) \+ reheat sweet potato\. Restocks the back half of the week\.

# 5\. Weekly Shopping List \(~£55–65\)

- Chicken breast 1 kg — £6–8
- Lean turkey mince 500 g — £4–5
- Pork loin 600 g — £5–7
- Rump or sirloin 350 g \(2 portions\) — £6–8
- Eggs 12\-pack — £2\.50
- Lean back bacon 6 rashers — £2
- Halloumi 225 g — £2\.50
- Greek yoghurt 0% 1 kg — £2\.50
- Cottage cheese 500 g — £2
- Semi\-skimmed milk 2 L — £2
- Cheddar 200 g — £2\.50
- Oats 1 kg — £1\.50
- Basmati rice 1 kg — £2
- Wholemeal wraps × 6 — £1\.50
- Sweet potato 1 kg, new potatoes 1 kg — £3
- Broccoli, asparagus, cabbage, apple, salad, tomato, onion, peppers — £8
- Berries, banana — £4
- Black beans, sweetcorn, salsa, hummus tinned/jarred — £4
- Olive oil, peri\-peri, fajita seasoning, Dijon — replenish monthly
- Whey protein 1 kg — £25 \(lasts ~5 weeks\)

# 6\. Daily Movement \(NEAT\)

NEAT \(non\-exercise activity thermogenesis\) is calories burned outside of formal training — walking, fidgeting, standing\. For someone training 3–4×/week, NEAT is the difference between an effective surplus and accidental fat gain\. Target 7,000–10,000 steps/day, tracked on the Galaxy Watch\. Below 5,000 means the surplus turns into fat faster than muscle\. Above 12,000 daily eats into recovery if not matched with food\.

# 7\. Behavioural Rules

__Caffeine cutoff__

Half\-life ~5 h \(3–7 h individual\)\. Last caffeine ≥8 h before target sleep time\. For an 11 pm bedtime, that means no coffee, pre\-workout, or caffeinated tea after 3 pm\. Drysdale 2024 \(T2 RCT\) showed 400 mg caffeine 6 h pre\-bed reduced total sleep time by ~45 min\. Sleep loss directly impairs hypertrophy and recovery\.

__Alcohol__

Empty calories \(7 kcal/g\)\. Suppresses muscle protein synthesis ~37% for 24 h after a heavy session \(Parr 2014, T2 RCT\)\. Disrupts REM sleep architecture\. Cap: ≤4 units/week in Phase 1; zero during Phase 0 smoking quit \(largest relapse trigger\)\. Never within 24 h of a heavy training session\.

__Vitamin D — dose to your blood level__

Default 25 µg \(1,000 IU\)/day with a fat\-containing meal until you have a 25\(OH\)D blood test\. Then: <50 nmol/L use 75–100 µg/day for 8 weeks then retest; 50–75 nmol/L use 25–50 µg/day; >75 nmol/L use 25 µg/day maintenance\. UK winter \(Oct–Mar\) requires supplementation per NICE guidance regardless\. Do not exceed 100 µg \(4,000 IU\)/day without supervision\.

# 8\. Supplements \(Whole List\)

- Creatine monohydrate 5 g/day, any time, with anything\. ~£15/3 months\. T1 evidence for strength and lean mass\.
- Vitamin D3 — dosed per Section 7\. ~£5–10/3 months\.
- Whey protein — only if you cannot hit 120 g protein from food alone\. Unflavoured or vanilla, cheapest £/kg protein\.
- Nothing else\. No BCAAs, glutamine, test boosters, fat burners, or pre\-workouts beyond caffeine\.

# 9\. Common Mistakes

- Confusing raw and cooked weights\. Chicken loses ~25%, rice gains ~2\.5×\.
- Drinking calories accidentally — flavoured lattes, juice, smoothies\.
- Skipping Sunday prep "just this week" → adherence collapses Tuesday\.
- Weighing daily and reacting to noise\. Use 7\-day rolling average only\.
- Cutting carbs because of internet advice\. Carbs fuel training — eat them\.
- Over\-relying on protein bars instead of whole\-food protein\.
- Ignoring fibre — high\-protein diets cause constipation without 30 g/day\.

# 10\. Adjustment Rules \(Repeated from Tracking Guide\)

__Phase 0 \(maintenance, weeks 1–6\)__

- Stable ±0\.3 kg over 14 days → correct, hold\.
- Gaining >0\.3 kg/week → cut 100 kcal/day\.
- Losing >0\.3 kg/week → add 100 kcal/day\.

__Phase 1 \(lean bulk, weeks 7–26\)__

- Target 0\.25–0\.35 kg/week\.
- >0\.4 kg/week × 2 weeks → cut 100 kcal/day\.
- <0\.15 kg/week × 2 weeks → add 100 kcal/day\.
- Waist \+>2 cm/month without weight gain → cut 150 kcal/day\.
guides.training:
Training Guide: From Zero to Phase 1

*Jamie | Companion to action\_plan\.docx*

This is the beginner\-level training manual underneath the action plan\. It assumes you have never lifted before\. Read sections 1–4 before your first session; keep sections 5–7 open on your phone during training\.

# 1\. What You Are Trying to Achieve in a Session

Hypertrophy \(muscle growth\) happens when you expose a muscle to mechanical tension, close enough to failure that the last few reps feel genuinely hard, with enough volume \(total hard sets per week\) to drive adaptation\. Everything you will do serves this one principle\.

Your job in every set is simple: perform the movement with good technique, controlled tempo, and stop when you are 1–3 "reps in reserve" \(RIR\) from failure\. RIR 2 means you could have done 2 more reps before your form broke down\. This is the target for most of your sets in Phase 0\.

# 2\. Key Terms You Need to Know

- Rep \(repetition\): one full execution of a movement \(down \+ up = 1 rep\)\.
- Set: a group of reps done back to back without rest \(e\.g\. "3 sets of 10 reps"\)\.
- Rest between sets: 90–180 seconds for compounds, 60–120 for isolation\. Err on longer when in doubt\.
- RIR \(reps in reserve\): how many reps you could still do before failure\. RIR 2 = could do 2 more\. RIR 0 = failure\.
- Failure: the point at which you cannot complete another rep with good form\.
- Compound movement: multiple joints/muscles \(squat, deadlift, bench, row, overhead press\)\.
- Isolation movement: single joint \(curl, lateral raise, triceps extension\)\.
- Progressive overload: doing more over time \(more reps, more weight, or better form\) — the single driver of progress\.
- Tempo: speed of the movement\. Default = 2 seconds down \(eccentric\), 1 second up \(concentric\), no pause\. Controlled, not fast\.

# 3\. Universal Form Principles \(Apply to Every Lift\)

1. Brace your core before lifting — imagine bracing for a punch\. Keep it braced throughout\.
2. Neutral spine — no rounding of the lower back, no over\-arching\.
3. Control the weight on the way down\. Do not drop it\.
4. Full range of motion — go as deep as you can with good form, not as deep as your ego tells you\.
5. Breathe: exhale on the hard part \(lifting up\), inhale on the way down\.
6. Stop before form breaks\. A set ends the moment your technique degrades, not when you physically cannot move the weight\.
7. Film every new exercise from the side, compare to reference videos, adjust\. Do this for the first 2 weeks of every new movement\.

# 4\. Warm\-Up Protocol \(5–8 Minutes, Every Session\)

Skip this and you increase injury risk and reduce first\-set performance\. Do not skip\.

1. 2 minutes general warm\-up: march on the spot, arm circles, bodyweight squats \(10\), push\-ups on knees \(5\), cat\-cow \(5\)\.
2. 2 minutes movement\-specific mobility: hip circles, shoulder dislocates with a towel, thoracic rotations\.
3. For each main lift: 2 warm\-up sets at ~50% and ~75% of your working weight, 5 reps each\. Example: if working weight on bench is 16 kg DBs, warm\-up = 8 kg ×5, then 12 kg ×5, then 16 kg ×8–12\.
4. For accessory/isolation lifts: one light warm\-up set is enough\.

# 5\. Exercise Library \(Phase 0 Movements\)

For each exercise: setup, execution cues, common mistakes, reference video search terms\.

__Goblet Squat__

Setup: hold one dumbbell vertically at chest height, cupped in both hands under the top bell\. Feet shoulder\-width, toes slightly out\.

Execution: sit back and down, knees tracking over toes, descend until thighs at least parallel to floor\. Drive up through mid\-foot\. Keep chest up, back flat\.

Mistakes: knees caving in, heels lifting, rounding lower back, not going deep enough\.

Search: "goblet squat tutorial Jeff Nippard" or "goblet squat form Squat University"\.

__DB Bench Press__

Setup: sit on bench with dumbbells on thighs\. Lie back, kicking dumbbells up to shoulder level\. Feet flat on floor, slight arch in lower back, shoulder blades pulled down and back\.

Execution: lower dumbbells under control to chest level, slight bend at elbows \(~45° from torso, not flared 90°\)\. Press back up\. Do not lock out elbows hard\.

Mistakes: elbows flaring 90° \(shoulder strain\), bouncing off chest, pressing with feet off floor, dumbbells drifting apart at top\.

Search: "dumbbell bench press form Jeff Nippard"\.

__One\-Arm DB Row__

Setup: right knee and right hand on bench, left foot on floor, back parallel to ground\. Left hand holds dumbbell hanging straight down\.

Execution: pull dumbbell to hip, elbow driving back and up\. Squeeze shoulder blade\. Lower under control with full stretch\.

Mistakes: rotating torso, using biceps instead of back, short range of motion, jerking the weight\.

Search: "one arm dumbbell row form"\.

__DB Romanian Deadlift \(RDL\)__

Setup: dumbbells in hands in front of thighs, feet hip\-width, slight knee bend \(maintained throughout — this is NOT a squat\)\.

Execution: hinge at the hips, pushing hips backwards, lowering dumbbells along the front of your legs\. Keep back flat\. Descend until you feel a strong hamstring stretch \(usually just below the knee for most\)\. Drive hips forward to stand up\.

Mistakes: bending knees too much \(turns into a squat\), rounding lower back, dumbbells drifting away from legs, going too low \(below hamstring flexibility\)\.

Search: "dumbbell RDL form Jeff Nippard"\.

__DB Overhead Press \(Seated or Standing\)__

Setup: dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing forward, elbows slightly in front of body\. Seated on bench with back support is easier for a beginner\.

Execution: press dumbbells overhead until arms are straight \(do not lock out hard\)\. Lower under control to shoulder level\.

Mistakes: leaning back excessively, flaring elbows way out, bouncing at the bottom, pressing behind the head\.

Search: "seated dumbbell shoulder press form"\.

__Chin\-Up / Inverted Row__

If you cannot do a chin\-up yet: do inverted rows under a low bar \(or use a resistance band assistance\)\. Progress to full chin\-ups over weeks\.

Chin\-up setup: palms facing you, shoulder\-width grip, hang with straight arms\.

Execution: pull until chin over bar, squeezing lats and biceps\. Lower under control to full hang\.

Mistakes: kipping \(swinging\), half reps, not controlling descent\.

Search: "chin up progression for beginners Jeff Nippard"\.

__DB Split Squat__

Setup: dumbbells at sides, step one foot forward into a long lunge position\. Back heel off floor\.

Execution: lower straight down until back knee nearly touches floor\. Drive up through front heel\. Do all reps on one leg, then switch\.

Mistakes: leaning too far forward, front knee caving in, pushing off back foot too much \(robs the front leg of work\)\.

Search: "dumbbell split squat form"\.

__DB Hip Thrust__

Setup: upper back on bench, feet flat on floor, dumbbell across hips \(use a pad or towel for comfort\)\. Knees bent ~90° at the top\.

Execution: drive hips up until body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders\. Squeeze glutes hard at the top\. Lower under control\.

Mistakes: hyperextending lower back at top, using lower back instead of glutes, not reaching full lockout\.

Search: "dumbbell hip thrust form Bret Contreras"\.

__DB Deadlift \(Conventional\)__

Setup: dumbbells on floor at sides of feet, feet hip\-width, bend at hips and knees to grip them\. Back flat, chest up, shoulders slightly in front of dumbbells\.

Execution: drive through floor with legs first, then extend hips at the top\. Lower by hinging at hips first, then bending knees once dumbbells pass knees\.

Mistakes: rounding back, jerking off the floor, hyperextending at top, making it a squat\.

Search: "dumbbell deadlift form"\.

__DB Incline Press__

Setup: bench at 30–45° incline\. Same as flat bench otherwise\.

Execution: same as flat bench\. Slightly more shoulder involvement, slightly less lower pec\. Stop just short of lockout\.

Mistakes: too steep an incline \(becomes a shoulder press\), flaring elbows\.

__DB Lateral Raise__

Setup: dumbbells at sides, slight elbow bend held throughout\.

Execution: raise dumbbells out to sides until arms parallel to floor\. Lead with elbows, not hands\. Lower slowly \(3 seconds down\)\. Use light weight — this is an isolation exercise\.

Mistakes: using momentum, going too heavy, shrugging shoulders up\.

Search: "dumbbell lateral raise form"\.

__Chest\-Supported DB Row__

Setup: lie face\-down on an incline bench \(~30°\), dumbbells hanging\.

Execution: row both dumbbells up to ribs, squeezing shoulder blades\. Control the descent\.

Mistakes: lifting chest off bench, short range\.

__DB Curl / Hammer Curl__

Setup: standing, dumbbells at sides\. Palms up for curl; palms facing each other for hammer curl\.

Execution: curl up, keeping elbows tucked at sides\. Control the descent\. Full range\.

Mistakes: swinging body to lift the weight, elbows drifting forward\.

__DB Triceps Extension / Skullcrusher__

Extension \(standing, 2 hands, dumbbell behind head\): lower dumbbell behind head until forearms parallel to floor, press back up\. Elbows stay narrow and point forward\.

Skullcrusher \(lying on bench\): hold two dumbbells arms extended above you, lower by bending at elbows only until dumbbells near forehead, press back up\.

Mistakes: flaring elbows, using shoulders to move the weight\.

__Plank__

Forearms on floor, body in straight line, core braced, glutes squeezed\. Hold for time \(30–60 s\)\. Do not let hips sag or pike up\.

__Hanging Knee Raise__

Hang from pull\-up bar, controlled raise of knees to chest, controlled lower\. No swinging\.

# 6\. Phase 0 Full\-Body Programme \(Weeks 1–6, 3×/week\)

Run this for 6 weeks\. Mon/Wed/Fri or any non\-consecutive days\. Expect each session to take 45–60 minutes including warm\-up\.

__Session A — Monday__

Warm\-up \(5–8 min as above\)

- 1\. Goblet squat — 3 sets × 8–12 reps, rest 120 s
- 2\. DB bench press — 3 × 8–12, rest 120 s
- 3\. One\-arm DB row — 3 × 8–12 each side, rest 90 s
- 4\. DB Romanian deadlift — 3 × 8–12, rest 120 s
- 5\. Plank — 3 × 30–45 s, rest 60 s

__Session B — Wednesday__

Warm\-up

- 1\. DB split squat — 3 × 10 each leg, rest 90 s
- 2\. Seated DB overhead press — 3 × 8–12, rest 120 s
- 3\. Chin\-up or inverted row — 3 × AMRAP \(as many reps as possible with good form\), rest 120 s
- 4\. DB hip thrust — 3 × 12–15, rest 90 s
- 5\. Hanging knee raise — 2 × AMRAP, rest 60 s

__Session C — Friday__

Warm\-up

- 1\. DB deadlift — 3 × 6–10, rest 150 s
- 2\. DB incline press — 3 × 8–12, rest 120 s
- 3\. Chest\-supported DB row — 3 × 10–12, rest 90 s
- 4\. DB lateral raise — 2 × 12–15, rest 60 s
- 5\. Superset: DB curl 2 × 10–15 \+ DB triceps extension 2 × 10–15, rest 60 s between supersets

# 7\. Phase 1 Upper/Lower Programme \(Weeks 7–26, 4×/week\)

Same exercises you know from Phase 0 plus variations\. Sessions take 60–75 minutes\.

__Upper A — Monday__

- DB bench press — 3 × 6–10
- One\-arm DB row — 3 × 8–12 each
- Seated DB overhead press — 3 × 8–12
- Chin\-up or inverted row — 3 × AMRAP
- DB curl — 2 × 10–15
- DB triceps extension — 2 × 10–15

__Lower A — Tuesday__

- Goblet or DB front squat — 3 × 8–12
- DB Romanian deadlift — 3 × 8–12
- DB split squat — 3 × 10 each leg
- Standing DB calf raise — 3 × 12–20
- Hanging knee raise — 3 × AMRAP

__Upper B — Thursday__

- DB incline press — 3 × 8–12
- Chin\-up or DB pullover — 3 × 8–12
- DB lateral raise — 3 × 12–20
- Chest\-supported DB row — 3 × 10–15
- DB hammer curl — 2 × 10–15
- DB skullcrusher — 2 × 10–15

__Lower B — Friday__

- DB deadlift — 3 × 6–10
- Front\-foot\-elevated split squat — 3 × 8–12 each leg
- DB hip thrust — 3 × 12–20
- Standing DB calf raise — 3 × 12–20
- Plank — 3 × 45 s

# 8\. How to Progress Week by Week

This is the most important section\. Most beginners fail at progression, not effort\.

1. Pick a rep range for each exercise \(e\.g\. 8–12 for bench press\)\.
2. Session 1: find a weight where you can do ~8 reps at RIR 2 on your first working set\. Use that weight for all 3 sets \(reps will drop on later sets — that is fine\)\.
3. Each session, aim to add 1 rep to at least one set on each exercise\. Example: week 1 you get 8/8/7 on bench; week 2 aim for 9/8/7 or 8/9/7, etc\.
4. When all 3 sets hit the TOP of the range \(12 reps each\), next session add ~2 kg and reset to the bottom of the range\. Example: you hit 12/12/12 at 16 kg — next session do 18 kg for 8–10 reps\.
5. Log every set in a notebook or app\. Without a log, you are guessing\.
6. Some weeks you will not progress\. That is normal, not a failure\. Push the next week\.

# 9\. Troubleshooting

__I cannot do a single chin\-up__

Normal at your starting point\. Do inverted rows \(body under a low bar, pull chest to bar\) for 4–8 weeks first\. Or use a resistance band looped over the bar and under one foot\. Progress to negatives \(jump up, lower slowly for 3–5 seconds\)\. Build up to one full chin\-up, then work to 3×5, then 3×AMRAP\.

__A specific exercise hurts \(joint pain, not muscle soreness\)__

Stop that exercise\. Pain is a signal\. Check form via video comparison\. Sub in an alternative \(e\.g\. if DB bench hurts shoulders, try floor press or push\-ups\)\. If pain persists >1 week, see a physio\.

__I am too sore to train the next session__

Expected in weeks 1–2\. Train through mild soreness \(DOMS\) — it dissipates once you warm up\. If soreness is severe and still present after 48 hours, you did too much volume — drop one set per exercise next session\.

__I do not feel the target muscle working__

Most common fix: slow the tempo \(3 s down, 1 s up, 1 s squeeze\), use lighter weight, focus on the muscle contracting rather than moving the weight\. Mind\-muscle connection improves with practice\.

__I am not progressing after 4 weeks__

Audit: sleep, protein, calories, effort \(RIR honesty\), session adherence, log accuracy\. One of these is almost certainly the problem, not programme design\.

__Missed a session__

Skip it, do not double up\. Get back on schedule the next day\.

# 10\. Safety Rules \(Non\-Negotiable\)

- Never train through sharp pain\. Dull muscle soreness is fine\.
- Never train without a proper warm\-up, even if pressed for time\.
- Never use a weight you cannot control eccentrically \(on the way down\)\.
- Never hold your breath through the whole set — breathe\.
- Do not train to failure on every set as a beginner\. Build the base first, push failure later\.
- If you feel dizzy, light\-headed, or chest pain during training — stop immediately, seek medical assessment if it persists or recurs\.
- No alcohol within 24 hours of training if possible \(blunts recovery\)\.
- Wear decent shoes \(flat\-soled for squats/deadlifts, running shoes are OK for upper\-body days\)\.
