Tracking & Weekly Review Guide

*Jamie | Companion to action\_plan\.docx*

What to track, how to track it without losing your mind, and how to run a weekly review that turns data into decisions\.

# 1\. Tracking Philosophy

Track the minimum that enables decisions\. Over\-tracking becomes a substitute for doing\. Under\-tracking leaves you guessing about why progress stalled\. The goal is a weekly loop: log → review → adjust → continue\.

# 2\. Daily Log \(2 Minutes, Morning\)

Do this every morning immediately after weighing\.

- Body weight \(post\-void, pre\-food, pre\-drink, in underwear, on the same scale\)
- Sleep duration from Galaxy Watch
- Wake time
- Cigarettes yesterday \(target 0\)
- Subjective recovery 1–10 \(how you feel\)
- Planned training today \(Y/N\)

# 3\. During\-Training Log

For each exercise, log: weight used, reps per set, RIR estimate on last set\. Example: "DB Bench Press 16 kg — 10, 9, 8 | RIR 2"\. A paper notebook or app \(Strong, Hevy, free versions are fine\)\.

# 4\. Daily Nutrition Log

Use MacroFactor, Cronometer, or MyFitnessPal\. For the first 2 weeks, weigh and log every food\. After that, log by meal template \(e\.g\. "Meal 1 option A"\) and only weigh novel foods\.

- Protein total \(the only non\-negotiable number\)
- Calorie total \(approximate after week 2 is fine\)
- Water intake

# 5\. Weekly Log \(Sunday Morning, 10 Minutes\)

- 7\-day weight average vs previous 7\-day average
- Waist circumference at navel \(relaxed, after exhale\)
- Photos: front, side \(both\), back — same lighting, same underwear, same pose, natural light where possible
- Training sessions completed this week vs planned
- Protein\-hit days this week \(target ≥6/7\)
- Sleep regularity: SD of sleep midpoint across 7 nights
- Any red flags \(pain, illness, skipped meals, relapses\)

# 6\. The Sunday Review Protocol

15 minutes, Sunday morning, same time each week\.

1. Pull weekly numbers from daily logs\.
2. Compare to last week on four metrics: weight trend, waist, adherence %, strength on 2 key lifts\.
3. Apply decision rules \(see Section 7\)\.
4. Adjust inputs for next week if indicated\.
5. Note any qualitative observations: energy levels, mood, sleep quality, training enjoyment\.
6. Plan the week ahead: training days, meal prep day, any social events that will disrupt routine\.

# 7\. Decision Rules

__Phase 0 \(maintenance\)__

- If 14\-day weight trend is stable ±0\.3 kg → maintenance is correct, continue\.
- If gaining >0\.3 kg/week → cut 100 kcal/day\.
- If losing >0\.3 kg/week → add 100 kcal/day\.
- If protein\-hit days <5/7 → focus next week on meal prep, not calorie tweaks\.
- If adherence <80% on training → find and fix the friction before week 2 repeats\.

__Phase 1 \(lean bulk\)__

- Target: 0\.25–0\.35 kg/week gain\.
- If >0\.4 kg/week for 2 weeks → cut 100 kcal/day\.
- If <0\.15 kg/week for 2 weeks → add 100 kcal/day\.
- If waist gained >2 cm in a month without weight gain → cut 150 kcal/day\.
- If strength stalled for 3 weeks → audit sleep, protein, effort, programme adherence, in that order\.

__Phase 2 \(mini\-cut\)__

- Target: 0\.4–0\.6 kg/week loss\.
- If >0\.8 kg/week → add 100 kcal/day \(too fast, LBM at risk\)\.
- If strength drops >10% on any key lift → slow the cut or end early\.
- If duration exceeds 12 weeks → end cut regardless of goals, run 2 weeks at maintenance\.

# 8\. Red Flags That Interrupt the Routine

- Resting HR sustained >85 bpm without cause
- SpO2 dips below 88% overnight
- Sharp joint pain during or after training
- Unexplained weight loss or gain beyond adjustment rules
- Persistent low mood, anhedonia, or suicidal ideation
- Chest pain or shortness of breath during training
- Smoking relapse

Any of these: stop, reassess, consult GP if clinically relevant\.

# 9\. Photos: How to Take Them Properly

1. Same time of day \(morning, fasted, post\-void\)\.
2. Same lighting \(natural daylight from the front, not overhead\)\.
3. Same clothing \(underwear only, same pair\)\.
4. Same background \(plain wall\)\.
5. Same poses: relaxed front, relaxed side \(left AND right\), relaxed back\. Then flexed front for reference\.
6. Weekly, Sunday mornings\.
7. Store in a dedicated folder labelled by date \(YYYY\-MM\-DD\)\.

Photos are your single best measure of visible progress\. Scale weight lies on short timescales; photos do not\.

# 10\. When NOT to Adjust

Do not adjust calories, programme, or supplements based on:

- A single daily weight reading
- One bad training session
- Fewer than 10 days of data
- How you feel "today"
- Social media before/after photos
- A new study you read online \(run it past me first\)

The plan works on 14\-day rolling averages for a reason\. Trust the process\.
guides.addendum:
Addendum: Equipment, Cardio, Tracking System

*Jamie | v1 08/04/2026 | Supersedes equipment/cardio/tracking sections of training\_guide, action\_plan, tracking\_guide*

This addendum captures decisions reached after the v1 docs were written\. It covers the home gym equipment sequence, the cardio prescription, concurrent training rules, mobility/sprint additions, and the unified tracking system \(the Jamie PWA\)\. Where this addendum and an earlier doc conflict, this addendum wins until the v2 docs are merged\.

# 1\. Home Gym Equipment Sequence

Goal: optimal stack for hypertrophy and aesthetics with no money/space constraint, built in two waves so the early purchases get used while later ones are added once they are needed\.

__Wave 1 — month 0 \(~£900–1,400\)__

- Adjustable dumbbells to 40 kg per hand\. Bowflex SelectTech 1090, MX Select MX85, or Powerblock Elite EXP\. ~£600–900\. Spine of the gym for the first year regardless of what else you own\. Do not buy 24 kg or 32\.5 kg sets — outgrown inside 12 months on rows and presses\.
- Adjustable bench, flat\-incline\-decline, rated 250 kg\+\. Mirafit M2, Bowflex 5\.1S\. ~£100–180\. Unlocks incline DB press, chest\-supported rows, hip thrusts, split squats, seated DB press\.
- Power tower \(combined dip station \+ pull\-up bar \+ optional knee\-raise pads\)\. Mirafit M100, JLL PT300, Bowflex BodyTower\. ~£100–200\. Best ROI piece after the DBs — covers dips and weighted chin\-ups, the two highest\-yield bodyweight movements you can load progressively\.
- Dipping belt with chain \(~£20\) and a doorframe pull\-up bar as backup \(~£20\)\.

Wave 1 alone is a complete Phase 0 \+ early Phase 1 setup\. You can run the entire Phase 0 full\-body programme and the first weeks of Phase 1 upper/lower without anything else\.

__Wave 2 — month 3 \(~£1,500–2,400\)__

- Functional trainer with ≥90 kg stack and dual adjustable pulleys\. Mirafit M3, Gravity FT2000, REP Ares, Force USA G3\. ~£1,200–2,000\. Buy this when you have learned the movement patterns and want variation, isolation work, and constant tension\. By month 3 you will know which cable movements you actually want \(lateral raises, face pulls, cable rows, triceps pushdowns, pallof press, cable curls\) rather than buying it on theory\.
- Trap bar \(open hex\) plus 140 kg of bumper plates\. Mirafit, Strength Shop, Bulldog Gear\. ~£250–400\. Solves the one thing the functional trainer cannot — heavy axial\-loaded lower\-body work\. Trap bar deadlifts replace conventional deadlifts with lower injury risk and let you go heavy without a rack or spotter\.
- Resistance bands set, ankle straps, lifting straps\. ~£40 total\.

__Total full stack: ~£2,300–3,700\.__

Optimal home gym for hypertrophy and aesthetics\. You will not gain meaningfully more from any additional equipment for at least 2 years\.

__What is deliberately NOT in the stack and why__

- Smith machine — constrains bar path, worse than free weights and cables for hypertrophy \(Schoenfeld 2014, T2\)\.
- Full squat rack with barbell — only worth it if you specifically want to back squat / bench / OHP heavy with a barbell\. For aesthetics, the trap bar covers it\. Revisit at year 2 if you want to add a strength block for bone density \(Watson 2018 LIFTMOR, T2 RCT, T1\-style outcomes for BMD in postmenopausal women — extrapolated cautiously to younger males\)\.
- Concept2 StrengthErg — concentric\-only, ~5–15% hypertrophy penalty vs free weights at matched effort, no used market, does not replace heavy axial loading\. Real product, valid alternative for someone wanting maximum safety, but not the optimal pick for you\.
- Leg press, leg extension/curl machine, pec deck, calf raise machine — replicated by the stack above with better transfer or equivalent results\.

__Movement\-pattern slot mapping__

- Lower body: trap bar deadlift \(heavy compound\), DB Bulgarian split squat \(unilateral\), DB hip thrust on bench / cable pull\-through \(posterior chain isolation\), goblet squat or cable squat \(quad\-dominant accessory\)\.
- Horizontal press: DB bench flat and incline \(primary\), weighted dips \(secondary\), cable fly \(isolation finisher\)\.
- Horizontal pull: chest\-supported DB row on incline bench, cable seated row at multiple heights, cable face pull for rear delts\.
- Vertical press: seated DB shoulder press, cable lateral raise \(constant tension is the key reason cables beat DBs here\), DB front raise as needed\.
- Vertical pull: weighted chin\-ups \(primary\), cable lat pulldown \(secondary, lets you train to failure without grip dropout\)\.
- Arms and core: cable curl, cable triceps pushdown, cable pallof press, cable woodchop, hanging knee raise on the power tower\.

Every movement pattern has a free\-weight primary and a cable variant\. That is the point of the combined stack — best of both, never plateau because you cannot load something\.

# 2\. Cardio — Why, How Much, When to Start

Cardio was missing from the v1 plan\. It should not have been\. VO2 max is the single strongest predictor of all\-cause mortality in healthy adults — Mandsager 2018 \(T3, JAMA, n=122,007, 8\.4 y follow\-up\) showed the difference between low and elite VO2 max was a hazard ratio of 5\.04, larger than smoking, diabetes, or end\-stage renal disease\. For a 30\-year\-old quitting smoking, building cardiovascular capacity is arguably the highest\-leverage health intervention you can make, separate from the muscle goal\.

__Prescription__

- Volume: ~90 min/week total, capped to protect hypertrophy\.
- Composition: 2× Zone 2 sessions \(30 min each, conversational pace, ~60–70% HRmax\) \+ 1× short interval session \(~20 min including warm\-up\)\.
- Interval protocol — 10\-20\-30 \(Gunnarsson 2012, T2\): 30 s easy, 20 s moderate, 10 s all\-out, ×5 per block, 3 blocks with 2 min rest between\. Lower drop\-out risk than Norwegian 4×4\. Progress to 4×4 \(Helgerud 2007, T2\) at month 3 if you want bigger VO2 max gains\.
- Schedule: separate from leg days by ≥6 hours where possible\. Cardio on rest days or after upper\-body sessions is ideal\.

__Timing — start week 4 of Phase 0__

You are quitting smoking in weeks 1–3 of Phase 0\. Cardio while in acute nicotine withdrawal is brutal and increases relapse risk\. Weeks 1–3: lifting \+ walking only \(NEAT target 7–10k steps/day already in nutrition\_guide\)\. Week 4: introduce cardio gradually — start with 2× 20 min Zone 2, build to the full prescription \(2× 30 min Zone 2 \+ 1× 20 min intervals\) by week 6\.

__Equipment — DECISION PENDING__

The cardio kit decision is open\. Three live options:

- Concept2 BikeErg \(~£700–900 used\)\. Lowest interference with hypertrophy \(Wilson 2012 T1 meta — cycling effect size on hypertrophy interference −0\.07, statistically zero\)\. Indestructible, PM5 monitor, free ErgData app, no subscription\. Smaller footprint than rower\. Loud \(~70 dB fan whoosh at hard effort\)\. Best objective pick for your goals\.
- Concept2 RowErg \(~£700–900 used\)\. Better cardiovascular stimulus per minute \(~85% muscle mass engaged vs ~50% cycling\)\. But meaningful interference with posterior\-chain lifting \(rowing fatigues lats, erectors, hamstrings, glutes\)\. Pick this only if you weight cardio higher than hypertrophy\.
- Keiser M3i \+ Peloton app \(~£1,400 used \+ £13/month\)\. Silent magnetic resistance, commercial\-grade build, classes for adherence\. ~£600 more than BikeErg\. Choose if adherence is your biggest risk\.

Recommendation: BikeErg, but pending your decision\. Whatever you pick, buy used in week 2 of Phase 0 so it is in place by week 3 and ready to use in week 4\.

__Concurrent training rules__

- Cap cardio at ~90 min/week total during hypertrophy phases\.
- Prioritise Zone 2 over HIIT \(Zone 2 has lower interference with strength\)\.
- Separate hard cardio from leg sessions by ≥6 hours\.
- Cycling interferes least with hypertrophy; rowing more; running most \(Wilson 2012, T1\)\.
- If a cardio session leaves you unable to hit your training numbers the next day, the cardio dose is too high — cut by 25% and reassess in 2 weeks\.

# 3\. Mobility, Carries, and Sprints

Three additions the v1 programme did not prescribe and should have\.

__Mobility \(~10 min post\-session, every session\)__

- Foam roller \(~£15\) and lacrosse ball \(~£5\)\.
- Hips: 90/90 hip stretch, half\-kneeling hip flexor, pigeon\. 1 min per side\.
- Thoracic spine: foam\-roller T\-spine extensions, open\-book stretch\. 1 min each\.
- Shoulders: cable face pulls \(already in programme\), wall slides, banded shoulder dislocates\. 1 min each\.

At 30 you will get away with skipping this\. At 35 you will not\. Cost: ~£20 \+ 10 min/session\. Single highest longevity ROI of any programme addition\.

__Loaded carries \(2 sets at end of each lower\-body day\)__

- Farmer's carry: heaviest DBs, walk 30 m or until grip fails\. 2 sets\.
- Suitcase carry \(single DB, opposite side stays neutral\): 20 m per side\. 1 set per side\.

Builds grip, core stiffness, and posterior chain endurance\. Already covered by the Wave 1 kit, just needs prescribing\.

__Sprint/jump session \(1× per week, 10 min\)__

- 6× 30 m hill sprints with full recovery between \(90 s\+\)\.
- Or: 3 sets of 5 broad jumps \+ 5 box jumps \(30–40 cm box\)\.

Power output declines from ~30 onward faster than strength\. Maintaining the ability to sprint, jump, and produce force quickly is what separates someone who ages well from someone who does not\. Cost: zero\. 10 min/week\.

# 4\. Tracking System — The Jamie PWA

The v1 tracking guide assumed Word docs \+ standalone apps \(MacroFactor, Hevy, Samsung Health, Concept2 ErgData\)\. That is fine as a starting point but it splits content \(recipes, form cues, decision rules\) from logging \(sets, meals, weights\) across multiple silos\. The destination is a unified system where content and logging live in the same place, fully owned by you, no subscriptions, no third\-party dependency long\-term\. The destination is the Jamie PWA\.

__What it is__

A Progressive Web App\. Single self\-contained file \(jamie\.html \+ supporting assets\) that installs to your phone home screen with a custom icon, runs full\-screen with no browser chrome, works offline indefinitely, accesses Bluetooth \(BikeErg PM5\), camera \(barcode scanning, progress photos\), notifications \(rest timers, daily log reminders\), and stores all data locally in IndexedDB\. Functionally indistinguishable from a native app\. Owned end\-to\-end by you: no Play Store, no subscriptions, no platform dependency, no company that can shut it down\. If Anthropic or any other vendor goes bust tomorrow, the file still works\.

__What it will contain at v2 \(full\-featured\)__

- Today view: current phase \+ week, today's training session with last session's numbers, today's meal plan from the recipe rotation, supplement checklist, cardio prescription, step count\.
- Train view: exercise library with form cues and video search links, set/rep/RIR logger with rest timer, plate calculator, progression tracking\. Replaces Hevy\.
- Eat view: 8\-recipe library with cook mode, ingredient scaling, "log this meal" button\. Personal food database for novel foods, Open Food Facts barcode scanning\. Bayesian\-style maintenance estimator over 14\-day rolling weight trend\. Replaces MacroFactor\.
- Cardio view: BikeErg session prescription with timer, live PM5 data via Web Bluetooth \(cadence, watts, distance, HR\)\. Replaces Concept2 ErgData\.
- Log view: daily form for weight, sleep, cigarettes, recovery 1–10, last caffeine time, alcohol units\. Two minutes/day\.
- Review view: Sunday review checklist with auto\-filled rolling averages and decision rule outputs\. Phase\-specific\.
- Stats view: weight trend \+ 14\-day rolling average, protein\-hit days, training volume, sleep regularity, step count, cardio minutes, current phase progress\.
- Photos view: progress photo timeline, swipe between dates\.
- Samsung Health integration via Health Connect API \(Android\) for sleep, HR, SpO2, steps\. Read\-only — Samsung Health stays as the sensing layer because the Galaxy Watch is its hardware\.

__Why a PWA and not a native app__

For your use case there is nothing a native Android app can do that a PWA cannot\. Native app cost: 80–150 hours of build time, Play Store developer account, sign\-and\-distribute workflow, ongoing platform maintenance\. PWA cost: ~15–25 hours of build time, zero ongoing maintenance, more portable, more durable\. Twitter, Spotify, Starbucks, Pinterest, Uber all ship PWAs as their primary mobile experience for hundreds of millions of users\. PWA is the right tool, not a lesser one\.

__Build phases__

- Week 0 of Phase 0: install Tier A apps \(MacroFactor, Hevy, Samsung Health, ErgData\) as the interim stack\. Use them for real logging while v1 is being built\. /Research/exports/ folder created for weekly CSV exports\.
- Week 3 of Phase 0: jamie\.html v1 ships\. Includes Today, Train \(replaces Hevy\), Eat \(recipe library and "log this meal" buttons reading from MacroFactor CSV exports\), Cardio \(BikeErg prescription, Web Bluetooth integration if BikeErg is in by then\), Log, Review, Stats, Photos\. Drops Hevy and ErgData immediately\. MacroFactor remains as nutrition logger\.
- Week 8 of Phase 0/1 transition: jamie\.html v2 ships\. Adds full nutrition module — personal food database seeded with your top ~40 ingredients extracted from the MacroFactor history, Open Food Facts barcode scanning, public\-domain Bayesian\-style maintenance estimator, MacroFactor history JSON imported on first run so trends continue uninterrupted\. Drops MacroFactor\. Subscription savings ~£60/year\.
- Month 6: Samsung Health integration via Health Connect API if stable\. If not, daily CSV export from Samsung Health into /Research continues as the fallback\.
- Month 6\+: full Option 2 reached\. All four third\-party apps replaced or reduced to invisible sensing layers\. Total ongoing cost: zero\.

__Architecture decisions baked into v1__

- Migration\-ready schema: nutrition\_log entries have a "source" field \("macrofactor" | "manual"\)\. Same dashboards/trends/decision rules read from the unified log regardless of source\. On migration day, the source flips and no rewrites are needed\.
- IndexedDB for storage: gigabytes available, persists across PWA updates, can be exported to JSON for backup or for me to read in /Research\.
- Service worker for offline\-first: works without internet after first install\.
- Sync to /Research: on tap of "Export," the file generates a JSON dump of all logs and downloads it\. You drop the JSON in /Research and I read it directly\. Optional Syncthing or Google Drive sync for automatic backup\.
- Content\-as\-data: recipes, exercises, decision rules, phase definitions, supplement doses, form cues all live in a single JSON block at the top of the file\. Updating content = me editing the JSON and you pulling the new file\. Logs stay in IndexedDB and persist across content updates\.

__What this replaces and what it does not__

- Replaces: Hevy entirely, ErgData entirely, MacroFactor entirely \(at week 8\), Word docs as the primary content delivery \(the docs become reference/backup, the PWA becomes the daily driver\)\.
- Does not replace: Samsung Health \(it stays as the sensing layer because the Galaxy Watch hardware needs it\)\. The PWA reads from it via Health Connect or CSV export\.
- Loses vs MacroFactor: nothing functionally critical for your use case\. The proprietary refinement on edge cases \(rapid weight changes, missed\-logging weeks\) is real but small at your steady\-state\.
- Loses vs Hevy: nothing — Hevy's actual advantage is polish and social/sharing features you do not care about\.

__Honest risks__

- Build delays\. ~15–25 hours of my time spread over 8 weeks is the realistic estimate, but it could slip if features turn out harder than scoped\. Mitigation: v1 ships at week 3 even if it is missing some polish; iterate\.
- You hate using it\. If after 4 weeks of v1 the friction is higher than Hevy/MacroFactor, we pull back to the off\-the\-shelf stack permanently\. The v1 ship is the test, not the commitment\.
- Web Bluetooth flakiness\. Web Bluetooth on Android Chrome works but is occasionally janky with reconnections\. Fallback: manual entry of BikeErg session totals from the PM5 display\.
- Health Connect API maturity\. May not be stable enough at month 6\. Fallback: daily CSV export from Samsung Health, manual import into the PWA\.

# 5\. Updated Phase 0 Schedule \(Weeks 1–6\)

Weeks 1–3: lifting \(full\-body 3×/week per training\_guide\) \+ walking \(NEAT 7–10k steps/day per nutrition\_guide\) \+ smoking quit per action\_plan \+ sleep regularisation per clinical\_sleep\_guide\. No structured cardio\. Tier A apps \(MacroFactor \+ Hevy \+ Samsung Health \+ ErgData\) for logging\. Buy cardio kit in week 2 so it is delivered by week 3\.

Week 3: jamie\.html v1 ships\. Switch from Hevy and ErgData to the PWA for training and cardio logging\. Continue MacroFactor for nutrition\.

Week 4: introduce cardio\. 2× 20 min Zone 2 sessions on the cardio kit\. No intervals yet\.

Week 5: 2× 25 min Zone 2 \+ 1× 15 min easy intervals as a primer \(10\-20\-30 single block\)\.

Week 6: full prescription — 2× 30 min Zone 2 \+ 1× 20 min full 10\-20\-30 intervals \(3 blocks\)\.

Mobility \(10 min post\-session\) and loaded carries \(2 sets/lower\-body day\) start week 1\. Sprint/jump session \(10 min/week\) starts week 4 alongside cardio\.

# 6\. Verification and Open Items

- Cardio kit decision still open\. Confirm by end of week 1 of Phase 0\.
- evidence\_table\.xlsx will be updated with: Mandsager 2018 \(T3, VO2 max and mortality\), Helgerud 2007 \(T2, Norwegian 4×4\), Wilson 2012 \(T1 meta, concurrent training interference\), Gunnarsson 2012 \(T2, 10\-20\-30 intervals\), Watson 2018 LIFTMOR \(T2, heavy resistance and BMD\)\. PMIDs to be verified before adding\.
- jamie\.html v1 build to start at week 3 of Phase 0\. Scope and schema design before then\.
- training\_guide\.docx, action\_plan\.docx, tracking\_guide\.docx will be merged with this addendum into v2 docs once jamie\.html v1 is shipped and proven\.
guides.literature:
Literature Review: Aesthetic Outcomes for an Untrained 30\-Year\-Old Skinny\-Fat Male \(Home Training\)

*Prepared for: Jamie  |  Date of search: 07/04/2026  |  Evidence table: evidence\_table\.xlsx \(companion file\)*

This document is an evidence synthesis, not personal medical advice\. Clinical actions \(GP workup, sleep study, surgical consult\) should be taken with a clinician\. Citations marked "verified" were retrieved via PubMed in the research session; others are widely cited in the training/nutrition literature but were not re\-pulled in this session and should be re\-verified before any action that depends on them\.

# 1\. PICO

Population: untrained 30\-year\-old male, ~65\.8 kg, ~1\.73 m, BMI ~22, waist ~73–78 cm \(jean\-size proxy\), "skinny\-fat" presentation, longstanding \(>15 years\) gynaecomastia with probable glandular component, active smoker currently quitting, fragmented/variable sleep with witnessed snoring and possible apnoea\. No known endocrine disease; bloods not yet done\.

Intervention: progressive resistance training at home \(dumbbell\-based, scalable\), modest caloric surplus, high protein, sleep and smoking correction, conservative supplementation\.

Comparator: no training; alternative paradigms \(bodyweight\-only, cut\-first, recomposition, pharmacological augmentation\)\.

Outcome: primary — lean mass gain and aesthetic outcome at 6/12/18 months\. Secondary — chest appearance \(pseudo vs glandular resolution\), safety, sustainability, diagnostic clarity on gynaecomastia\.

Scope: mixed clinical, interventional, and mechanistic\. Key assumption surfaced: "skinny\-fat" at BMI 22 means low muscle mass rather than high fat — testable via waist and bloods\.

# 2\. Search Strategy

PubMed MCP queries \(07/04/2026\) across: training volume, frequency and proximity\-to\-failure; protein and hypertrophy; energy\-deficit recomposition; creatine; sleep restriction and testosterone; OSA and testosterone; semaglutide and tirzepatide body composition; bimagrumab and the activin pathway; tamoxifen and gynaecomastia; surgical gynaecomastia; smoking cessation and muscle\. Thirteen primary citations were directly verified in PubMed this session \(PMIDs listed in the companion evidence table\)\. The remainder are flagged "unverified this session" in the evidence table — they are real papers in widespread use, not fabrications, but warrant re\-verification before any decision that depends on their exact effect sizes\.

# 3\. Well\-Established Findings \(High to Moderate GRADE\)

## Training stimulus is the rate\-limiting factor

Resistance training to within ~0–3 reps of failure, with at least 10 hard sets per muscle group per week, produces hypertrophy in untrained men with large and reliable effect sizes \(Schoenfeld volume meta\-analysis, T1, PMID 27102172, moderate certainty\)\. Training frequency per se does not matter once volume is equated \(Schoenfeld 2019 frequency meta\-analysis, T1, PMID 30558493\)\. Proximity to failure matters modestly — training closer to failure confers a small hypertrophy advantage without a strength penalty \(Refalo 2024 meta\-analysis, T1, PMID 38970765, low\-to\-moderate certainty\)\.

Practical read: 3–4 sessions per week, 10–15 hard sets per muscle group per week, most sets taken to RIR 1–3, with the last set of a movement occasionally to failure\. You will access ~60–80% of possible hypertrophy adaptation just by doing this consistently\. Everything else in this review is a decorator on top of this foundation\.

## Protein at 1\.6–2\.2 g/kg/day is the nutritional ceiling for hypertrophy benefit

Morton et al\. 2018 \(T1, PMID 28698222, n=1863 across 49 RCTs, high certainty\): protein supplementation augments RT\-induced fat\-free mass \(FFM\) gain by \+0\.30 kg \(95% CI 0\.09–0\.52\), with a hard plateau at 1\.62 g/kg/day\. For you at 65\.8 kg: target 105–145 g/day, distributed across 3–4 meals of ~30–45 g each\. Source \(whole food vs whey\) does not meaningfully matter once the total is hit\. This is the single most\-evidenced nutritional variable in the review\.

## Creatine monohydrate is the only supplement worth taking by default

3–5 g/day, no loading required\. Multiple meta\-analyses converge on \+1 to \+1\.4 kg LBM vs placebo over 8–12 weeks of RT, plus small strength gains \(Chilibeck 2017; Kreider 2017 ISSN position stand, T1/T6, high certainty\)\. Non\-suppressive, long\-term safe, cheap \(~£15/month UK\)\.

## Sleep restriction and circadian disruption suppress testosterone and worsen p\-ratio

Leproult & Van Cauter 2011 \(T2, PMID 21632481, JAMA\): 5 h/night for 1 week in healthy young men reduced daytime testosterone 10–15%\. Nedeltcheva 2010 \(T2, Annals of Internal Medicine, widely cited\): sleep restriction in a weight\-loss intervention shifted loss from fat to lean mass \(55% more LBM lost, 55% less fat lost\) at equal total weight loss\. Small studies, but direction and magnitude are consistent\.

## Obstructive sleep apnoea independently suppresses testosterone

Meta\-analyses of OSA cohorts show total testosterone ~2–3 nmol/L lower than matched controls in moderate\-severe OSA, severity\-dependent \(T1/T4 mix, PubMed cluster including PMID 35904664\)\. CPAP improves this modestly and slowly \(Zhang 2019, T1, low certainty for the testosterone endpoint specifically — the bigger wins from CPAP are sleep architecture, cardiovascular risk, and daytime function\)\.

Action point: your self\-reported snoring with perceived apnoea is a positive OSA screen that warrants a STOP\-BANG and, if positive, a sleep study via GP\. This is the single most important thing to do this month\.

# 4\. Emerging / Probable \(Low to Moderate GRADE\)

## Body recomposition is achievable in untrained men in a modest deficit if protein is high

Longland 2016 \(T2, PMID 26817506\) remains the strongest single data point — n=40, 4 weeks, 40% deficit, 2\.4 g/kg protein \+ RT: \+1\.2 kg LBM and −4\.8 kg fat vs \+0\.1 kg and −3\.5 kg in the 1\.2 g/kg arm\. Duration is the major limitation \(4 weeks is not a cut\), and the effect does not clearly extrapolate to leaner starting points\. For you at BMI 22 this means recomposition is possible but not the best fit — you do not have enough fat to cut productively, and newbie\-gains physiology favours a mild surplus\.

## Lean bulk rate\-of\-gain for untrained men

No T1/T2 data directly establishing optimal surplus magnitude — this is an expert\-opinion area \(Helms, Iraki et al\., T6\)\. Convergent practice guidance: \+150–250 kcal/day, target ~0\.25–0\.4 kg/week weight gain, monitor over 14\-day rolling averages\. Faster than that and p\-ratio collapses — you accumulate fat without proportional muscle gain\. Low\-to\-moderate certainty, but the downside of the alternative \(aggressive surplus\) is well\-characterised\.

## Dumbbell\-based home training is functionally equivalent to barbell/machine training for hypertrophy

Direct head\-to\-head RCTs are scarce, but the mechanistic and volume\-matched evidence consistently shows that hypertrophy responds to mechanical tension and proximity\-to\-failure, not equipment category\. Confidence moderate for the first ~18–24 months of training; lower beyond that, because the loadable range on dumbbells caps out at the point where you would want a barbell\.

## Smoking cessation recovers muscle protein synthesis capacity

Smokers have blunted post\-prandial MPS response and impaired RT adaptations \(small mechanistic studies, T5/T3, low\-to\-moderate certainty\)\. Quitting now is strictly positive for your training outcomes\. Expect 4–6 weeks of withdrawal\-related sleep disruption before you see the sleep benefit\.

# 5\. Speculative / Hypothesis\-Generating \(Low to Very\-Low GRADE\)

## SERMs \(tamoxifen, raloxifene, clomiphene\) for hypertrophy in eugonadal men

Mechanism is real: tamoxifen raises total testosterone 30–45% in eugonadal men over weeks \(small T2/T3 studies\)\. However, no RCTs have tested SERM monotherapy with a hypertrophy or body\-composition primary endpoint in healthy young men\. The inferential leap from "testosterone \+35% within\-normal\-range" to "meaningful muscle gain" is not supported by Bhasin 1996 dose\-response logic — hypertrophy scales with supraphysiological androgen exposure, not physiological fluctuations\.

For gynaecomastia specifically: SERMs work only for early proliferative disease \(<12 months\), not established fibrotic disease of >15 years \(Lapid 2013 systematic review area, T3, low certainty\)\. Side\-effect ledger non\-trivial \(VTE, ocular, lipid\)\. Net: not recommended\. Very low certainty it would help your primary goals; moderate certainty it would not help your chest\.

## GLP\-1 receptor agonists \(semaglutide, tirzepatide\)

Wilding 2021 STEP\-1 \(NEJM, T2, n=1961, −14\.9% body weight at 68 weeks\) and Jastreboff 2022 SURMOUNT\-1 \(NEJM, T2, n=2539, up to −20\.9%\) are landmark — high certainty for weight loss in obese adults\. The lean mass cost is real: DEXA substudies show ~25–40% of lost weight is LBM\. At BMI 22 you are not a trial\-eligible population and the lean mass cost directly opposes your goal\. Not recommended now\. Potentially relevant in a year\-2 cut phase if appetite is the binding constraint, only with aggressive RT and high protein\.

## Myostatin/activin pathway \(bimagrumab, apitegromab, follistatin\-based\)

Heymsfield 2021 \(T2, PMID 33439265, n=75, bimagrumab in obese type 2 diabetes, 48 weeks\): LBM \+2\.5–3 kg and fat −7\.5 kg\. This is the most interesting non\-androgen signal in the pipeline — simultaneous lean gain and fat loss is unusual\. Not yet approved anywhere as of April 2026\. Not accessible outside trials\. Phase III ongoing\. Worth monitoring; do not wait for it\.

## Grey\-market peptides \(BPC\-157, TB\-500, GH secretagogues\)

BPC\-157 and TB\-500: zero human RCTs for muscle, recovery, or hypertrophy — absence\-of\-evidence, not a "probably works" finding\. GH secretagogues do raise endogenous GH/IGF\-1, but Liu 2008 meta\-analysis \(T1, Annals of Internal Medicine, rhGH in healthy athletes\) showed that even exogenous GH produces only fluid\-driven LBM gains with no strength or performance benefit\. Unlicensed UK/EU, grey\-market sourcing with unknown purity and long\-term safety\. Strongly not recommended\.

## SARMs

Ostarine, LGD\-4033, RAD\-140 and similar are frequently marketed as non\-suppressive\. The clinical trial data \(Dalton 2011 ostarine phase II; Basaria 2013 LGD phase I, both T2\) show dose\-dependent LH/FSH/testosterone suppression even at low doses\. They fail your non\-suppressive criterion\. This is the most common piece of misinformation in this space\.

# 6\. Steelman of the Counter\-Case

The strongest opposing view is: "You are being over\-cautious\. A 30\-year\-old with newbie gains on the table should push hard — aggressive surplus, high volume from day one, add in a low\-dose SARM or tamoxifen cycle, and the chest issue resolves itself once you are muscular enough to disguise it\." This view has partial truth in the volume and surplus dimensions \(you will grow faster if you push harder\) but falls apart on three empirical points:

\(1\) p\-ratio in an aggressive surplus on fragmented sleep is dreadful, documented across the sleep\-restriction literature — you will gain fat faster than muscle and end the year looking worse\.

\(2\) SARMs are suppressive and tamoxifen has no hypertrophy evidence, so the pharmacological lever is mostly placebo at real cost\.

\(3\) Established glandular gynaecomastia does not hide behind pec development — it can look worse at lower body fat with more pec development beneath it\.

What would falsify my recommendation: a clean sleep study showing no OSA, demonstrated >0\.4 kg/week LBM gain sustained over 8 weeks on a \+300 kcal surplus with no fat gain >0\.1 kg/week, and chest appearance improving with fat loss \(suggesting the glandular component is smaller than I am assuming\)\. Any of those would loosen the protocol\.

# 7\. Recommended Protocol

## Phase 0, weeks 1–6: Diagnose and Stabilise

Clinical\. Book GP this week\. Request: endocrine workup for longstanding gynaecomastia \(total and free testosterone, oestradiol sensitive assay, LH, FSH, prolactin, SHBG, hCG, TSH, LFTs, U&Es\); STOP\-BANG, and if ≥3, referral for sleep study \(NHS or private home study ~£150–250\); smoking cessation support\.

Sleep behaviour\. Fix wake time \(same every day ±30 min, weekends included\); let bedtime drift\. Sleep regularity outperforms sleep duration as a predictor of metabolic and mortality outcomes\. Turn on Galaxy Watch SpO2 tracking tonight; two weeks of data is the minimum useful sample\.

Nutrition\. Maintenance calories \(~2,500–2,700 kcal/day estimated at your stats and sedentary\-to\-light activity; refine from scale trend\)\. Protein 110–145 g/day\. Creatine 5 g/day from day one\. Vitamin D 10 µg/day \(UK PHE winter recommendation; test level at GP visit\)\. Nothing else\.

Training\. Start immediately, full\-body 3x/week, 8–10 hard sets per major muscle group per week, RIR 2–3 on most sets, equipment\-independent exercise selection\.

## Phase 1, weeks 7–26: Lean Bulk, Full Volume

Trigger for phase transition: sleep baseline characterised \(either clean study or CPAP started\), endocrine bloods back, smoking quit ≥4 weeks, Galaxy Watch showing stable regularity \(SD of sleep midpoint <60 min\)\.

Nutrition\. \+150–250 kcal over maintenance\. Target rate of gain 0\.25–0\.35 kg/week, monitored by 7\-day weight average\. Protein unchanged\. If 14\-day trend exceeds 0\.4 kg/week, cut 100 kcal; if under 0\.15 kg/week, add 100 kcal\.

Training\. Upper/lower 4x/week, 12–16 hard sets per muscle group per week, RIR 1–2 on most sets, last set of primary movements to failure every ~10 days\. Progressive overload tracked in a log — add reps within the target range, add load when the top of the range is reached\.

## Phase 2, week 27 onwards: Reassess

Lean bulk continues if rate and p\-ratio are on track\. Mini\-cut \(8–12 weeks, ~0\.5%/week\) if body fat has drifted above comfortable\. Gynaecomastia reassessed at lower body fat — at that point, a plastic surgery consult becomes a reasonable information\-gathering step \(not a commitment to operate\)\.

# 8\. Programme \(Phase 1 Template — Scale Down for Phase 0\)

__Upper A \(Monday\)__

DB bench press 3 × 6–10; one\-arm DB row 3 × 8–12; DB overhead press 3 × 8–12; chin\-up or inverted row 3 × AMRAP; DB curl 2 × 10–15; DB triceps extension 2 × 10–15\.

__Lower A \(Tuesday\)__

DB goblet squat 3 × 8–12; DB Romanian deadlift 3 × 8–12; DB split squat 3 × 10/leg; DB calf raise 3 × 12–20; hanging knee raise 3 × AMRAP\.

__Wednesday: Rest__

__Upper B \(Thursday\)__

DB incline press 3 × 8–12; chin\-up or DB pullover 3 × 8–12; DB lateral raise 3 × 12–20; DB row \(chest\-supported\) 3 × 10–15; DB hammer curl 2 × 10–15; DB skullcrusher 2 × 10–15\.

__Lower B \(Friday\)__

DB deadlift 3 × 6–10; DB front\-foot\-elevated split squat 3 × 8–12/leg; DB hip thrust 3 × 12–20; standing DB calf raise 3 × 12–20; plank 3 × 45 s\.

__Weekend: Rest__

Progression\. Fixed rep range per exercise; add 1 rep/week until top of range; when all sets hit top, add ~2 kg and drop reps to bottom of range\. Log every set\. For phase 0, remove the second chin\-up day, cut one accessory per session, and keep RIR 2–3 throughout\.

# 9\. UK Kit Shopping List

__Phase 0 minimum \(~£350–£450\)__

Adjustable dumbbells 2 × 32 kg \(Mirafit, JLL, Bowflex\-equivalent\)\. Single highest\-leverage purchase\. Ceiling of 32 kg/hand covers ~18–24 months of pressing progression and indefinitely for most accessories\.

Flat/incline adjustable bench \(FID or FI\)\. ~£80–£150 entry\-level; £150–£250 for something stable you will not need to replace\.

Doorway pull\-up bar \(£15–£30\) or wall/ceiling\-mounted bar \(£40–£80\) if a doorway is unsuitable\.

Cheap floor mat or 10 mm EVA tiles \(~£30\)\. Food scale and reliable body scale \(~£30\)\.

__Phase 1 upgrade when you move into the dedicated room \(\+£400–£800\)__

Adjustable dumbbells to 40 kg/hand, OR a barbell \+ plates \+ squat rack \+ bench combo\. The barbell route is better value and scales further if space allows\.

Pair of parallettes or dip station \(~£40–£80\)\. Resistance bands set for warm\-ups and assistance \(~£25\)\.

Deliberately not recommended: peptides, SERMs, SARMs, GLP\-1s, "test boosters\." None belong in year one for your profile\.
